001|# Product Requirement Document (PRD): python_gpt4_test_automation_app
002|
003|## 1. Language & Project Info
004|- **Language:** English
005|- **Programming Language:** Python
006|- **Project Name:** python_gpt4_test_automation_app
007|- **Restated Requirements:**
008|  - Develop a Python standalone .exe application that utilizes the GPT-4 API to:
009|    - Generate input Excel files for automated test scenarios
010|    - Analyze output files and categorize errors
011|    - Log all data into a RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) knowledge base
012|    - Improve future input generation based on historical data
013|    - Support multi-client environments
014|    - Use a local storage solution (SQLite or JSON)
015|
016|## 2. Product Definition
017|### Product Goals
018|1. Automate the generation and analysis of test scenario files using GPT-4.
019|2. Enable error categorization and continuous improvement of test inputs via a RAG knowledge base.
020|3. Support multi-client operations with secure, efficient local data storage.
021|
022|### User Stories
023|- As a QA engineer, I want to automatically generate test input Excel files so that I can save time and reduce manual errors.
024|- As a test manager, I want the application to analyze output files and categorize errors so that I can quickly identify and address issues.
025|- As a developer, I want all test data and error logs stored in a RAG knowledge base so that the system can improve future input generation.
026|- As an IT administrator, I want the application to support multiple clients so that different teams can use it independently.
027|- As a product owner, I want local storage using SQLite or JSON so that data is secure and easily accessible.
028|### Competitive Analysis
029|
030|| Product/Tool                | Pros                                              | Cons                                              |
031||-----------------------------|---------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------|
032|| TestRail                    | Robust test management, multi-client support      | No GPT-4 integration, limited automation          |
033|| Selenium                    | Widely used, open-source, flexible                | Manual test case creation, no built-in RAG        |
034|| Applitools                  | Visual testing, AI-powered error detection        | Focused on UI, not Excel or GPT-4                 |
035|| Katalon Studio              | All-in-one automation, supports data-driven tests | No GPT-4, limited error categorization            |
036|| Tricentis Tosca             | Model-based, strong analytics                     | Expensive, complex setup                          |
037|| Testim                      | AI-powered, easy integration                      | Limited local storage, no RAG knowledge base      |
038|| Custom Python Frameworks    | Highly customizable, open-source                  | Requires manual setup, lacks advanced analytics   |
039|
040|### Competitive Quadrant Chart
041|```mermaid
042|quadrantChart
043|    title "Automated Test Scenario Tools: Innovation vs. Integration"
044|    x-axis "Low Innovation" --> "High Innovation"
045|    y-axis "Low Integration" --> "High Integration"
046|    quadrant-1 "Expand Features"
047|    quadrant-2 "Promote Integration"
048|    quadrant-3 "Re-evaluate"
049|    quadrant-4 "Improve Innovation"
050|    "TestRail": [0.3, 0.8]
051|    "Selenium": [0.4, 0.6]
052|    "Applitools": [0.7, 0.5]
053|    "Katalon Studio": [0.5, 0.7]
054|    "Tricentis Tosca": [0.6, 0.9]
055|    "Testim": [0.8, 0.4]
056|    "python_gpt4_test_automation_app": [0.9, 0.8]
057|```

001|# Product Requirement Document (PRD): python_gpt4_test_automation_app
002|
003|## 1. Language & Project Info
004|- **Language:** English
005|- **Programming Language:** Python
006|- **Project Name:** python_gpt4_test_automation_app
007|- **Restated Requirements:**
008|  - Develop a Python standalone .exe application that utilizes the GPT-4 API to:
009|    - Generate input Excel files for automated test scenarios
010|    - Analyze output files and categorize errors
011|    - Log all data into a RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) knowledge base
012|    - Improve future input generation based on historical data
013|    - Support multi-client environments
014|    - Use a local storage solution (SQLite or JSON)
015|
016|## 2. Product Definition
017|### Product Goals
018|1. Automate the generation and analysis of test scenario files using GPT-4.
019|2. Enable error categorization and continuous improvement of test inputs via a RAG knowledge base.
020|3. Support multi-client operations with secure, efficient local data storage.
021|
022|### User Stories
023|- As a QA engineer, I want to automatically generate test input Excel files so that I can save time and reduce manual errors.
024|- As a test manager, I want the application to analyze output files and categorize errors so that I can quickly identify and address issues.
025|- As a developer, I want all test data and error logs stored in a RAG knowledge base so that the system can improve future input generation.
026|- As an IT administrator, I want the application to support multiple clients so that different teams can use it independently.
027|- As a product owner, I want local storage using SQLite or JSON so that data is secure and easily accessible.
028|### Competitive Analysis
029|
030|| Product/Tool                | Pros                                              | Cons                                              |
031||-----------------------------|---------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------|
032|| TestRail                    | Robust test management, multi-client support      | No GPT-4 integration, limited automation          |
033|| Selenium                    | Widely used, open-source, flexible                | Manual test case creation, no built-in RAG        |
034|| Applitools                  | Visual testing, AI-powered error detection        | Focused on UI, not Excel or GPT-4                 |
035|| Katalon Studio              | All-in-one automation, supports data-driven tests | No GPT-4, limited error categorization            |
036|| Tricentis Tosca             | Model-based, strong analytics                     | Expensive, complex setup                          |
037|| Testim                      | AI-powered, easy integration                      | Limited local storage, no RAG knowledge base      |
038|| Custom Python Frameworks    | Highly customizable, open-source                  | Requires manual setup, lacks advanced analytics   |
039|
040|### Competitive Quadrant Chart
041|```mermaid
042|quadrantChart
043|    title "Automated Test Scenario Tools: Innovation vs. Integration"
044|    x-axis "Low Innovation" --> "High Innovation"
045|    y-axis "Low Integration" --> "High Integration"
046|    quadrant-1 "Expand Features"
047|    quadrant-2 "Promote Integration"
048|    quadrant-3 "Re-evaluate"
049|    quadrant-4 "Improve Innovation"
050|    "TestRail": [0.3, 0.8]
051|    "Selenium": [0.4, 0.6]
052|    "Applitools": [0.7, 0.5]
053|    "Katalon Studio": [0.5, 0.7]
054|    "Tricentis Tosca": [0.6, 0.9]
055|    "Testim": [0.8, 0.4]
056|    "python_gpt4_test_automation_app": [0.9, 0.8]
057|```
058|## 3. Technical Specifications
059|
060|### Requirements Analysis
061|- The application must be a standalone .exe built with Python (e.g., using PyInstaller).
062|- It must integrate with the GPT-4 API for generating test input Excel files.
063|- The system should analyze output files, categorize errors, and log all data into a RAG knowledge base.
064|- The application must continuously improve input generation based on historical data and error analysis.
065|- Multi-client support is required, with data isolation and secure access for each client.
066|- Local storage must be implemented using either SQLite or JSON, configurable per deployment.
067|- The UI should be simple, allowing users to upload/download files, view error reports, and manage clients.
068|
069|### Requirements Pool
070|- **P0 (Must-have):**
071|  - Standalone .exe application
072|  - GPT-4 API integration for input generation
073|  - Output file analysis and error categorization
074|  - RAG knowledge base for logging and retrieval
075|  - Multi-client support with data isolation
076|  - Local storage (SQLite/JSON)
077|- **P1 (Should-have):**
078|  - Configurable error categories
079|  - User management and access control
080|  - Dashboard for error trends and input improvements
081|- **P2 (Nice-to-have):**
082|  - Customizable test scenario templates
083|  - Integration with external test management tools
084|  - Advanced analytics and reporting
085|
086|### UI Design Draft
087|- **Main Dashboard:**
088|  - Client selector (dropdown)
089|  - Upload input/output Excel files
090|  - Generate new test input (button)
091|  - View error reports (table/list)
092|  - Knowledge base access (search/view)
093|- **Settings:**
094|  - Storage type selection (SQLite/JSON)
095|  - API key management
096|  - User management (if enabled)
097|
098|### Open Questions
099|- What is the expected volume of test scenarios and clients?
100|- Are there specific security or compliance requirements for local storage?
101|- Should the RAG knowledge base support external integrations?
102|- What level of customization is needed for error categories and reporting?
103|- Is there a preferred framework for the UI (Tkinter, PyQt, etc.)?
(this is the end of the file)

multiple clients so that different teams can use it independently.\\n027|- As a product owner, I want local storage using SQLite or JSON so that data is secure and easily accessible.\\n028|### Competitive Analysis\\n029|\\n030|| Product/Tool                | Pros                                              | Cons                                              |\\n031||-----------------------------|---------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------|\\n032|| TestRail                    | Robust test management, multi-client support      | No GPT-4 integration, limited automation          |\\n033|| Selenium                    | Widely used, open-source, flexible                | Manual test case creation, no built-in RAG        |\\n034|| Applitools                  | Visual testing, AI-powered error detection        | Focused on UI, not Excel or GPT-4                 |\\n035|| Katalon Studio              | All-in-one automation, supports data-driven tests | No GPT-4, limited error categorization            |\\n036|| Tricentis Tosca             | Model-based, strong analytics 2025-9-20 14:17:44.141 | INFO     | metagpt.roles.di.role_zero:_act:294 - Commands outputs: 
Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/python_gpt4_test_automation_app/main.py has been successfully created, with content:
import sys
import tkinter as tk
from ui import UI
from client_manager import ClientManager
from storage import Storage
from config import Config

class Main:
    """
    Entry point for the python_gpt4_test_automation_app.
    Initializes UI, ClientManager, and Storage, and starts the application.
    """

    def __init__(self):
        # Load configuration
        self.config = Config()
        # Initialize storage (SQLite or JSON)
        self.storage = Storage(self.config.get_storage_type(), self.config.get_storage_path())
        # Initialize client manager
        self.client_manager = ClientManager(self.storage)
        # Initialize UI
        self.root = tk.Tk()
        self.ui = UI(
            root=self.root,
            client_manager=self.client_manager,
            storage=self.storage,
            config=self.config
        )

    def run(self):
        """
        Starts the application UI.
        """
        self.ui.start()
        self.root.mainloop()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    app = Main()
    app.run()

The app will use Tkinter for the UI (simple, cross-platform, and compatible with .exe builds). For GPT-4 API integration, we\'ll use the requests library. Excel file generation and analysis will be handled by openpyxl and pandas. Error categorization logic will be modular and configurable. The RAG knowledge base will use SQLite (default) or JSON for local storage, with per-client data isolation. Multi-client support will be managed via user authentication and client-specific data partitions. The system will continuously improve input generation by leveraging historical data and error logs stored in the knowledge base.\\n004|\\n005|## File list\\n006|\\n007|- main.py\\n008|- ui.py\\n009|- Support multi-client operations with secure, efficient local data storage.\\n021|\\n022|### User Stories\\n023|- As a QA engineer, I want to automatically generate test input Excel files so that I can save time and reduce manual errors.\\n024|- As a test manager, I want the application to analyze output files and categorize errors so that I can quickly identify and address issues.\\n025|- As a developer, I want all test data and error logs stored in a RAG knowledge base so that the system can improve future input generation.\\n026|- As an IT administrator, I want the application to support multiple clients so that different teams can use it independently.\\n027|- As a product owner, I want local storage using SQLite or JSON so that data is secure and easily accessible.\\n028|### Competitive Analysis\\n029|\\n030|| Product/Tool                | Pros                                              | Cons                                              |\\n031||-----------------------------|---------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------|\\n032|| TestRail                    | Robust test management, multi-client support      | No GPT-4 integration, limited automation          |\\n033|| Selenium                    | Widely used, open-source, flexible                | Manual test case creation, no built-in RAG        |\\n034|| Applitools                  | Visual testing, AI-powered error detection        | Focused on UI, not Excel or GPT-4                 |\\n035|| Katalon Studio              | All-in-one automation, supports data-driven tests | No GPT-4, limited error categorization            |\\n036|| Tricentis Tosca             | Model-based, strong analytics                     | Expensive, complex setup                          |\\n037|| Testim                      | AI-powered, easy integration                      | Limited local storage, no RAG knowledge base      |\\n038|| Custom Python Frameworks    | Highly customizable, open-source                  | Requires manual setup, lacks advanced analytics   |\\n039|\\n040|### Competitive Quadrant Chart\\n041|```mermaid\\n042|quadrantChart\\n043|    title "Automated Test Scenario Tools: Innovation vs. Integration"\\n044|    x-axis "Low Innovation" --> "High Innovation"\\n045|    y-axis "Low Integration" --> "High Integration"\\n046|    quadrant-1 "Expand Features"\\n047|    quadrant-2 "Promote Integration"\\n048|    quadrant-3 "Re-evaluate"\\n049|    quadrant-4 "Improve Innovation"\\n050|    "TestRail": [0.3, 0.8]\\n051|    "Selenium": [0.4, 0.6]\\n052|    "Applitools": [0.7, 0.5]\\n053|    "Katalon Studio": [0.5, 0.7]\\n054|    "Tricentis Tosca": [0.6, 0.9]\\n055|    "Testim": [0.8, 0.4]\\n056|    "python_gpt4_test_automation_app": [0.9, 0.8]\\n057|```\\n058|## 3. Technical Specifications\\n059|\\n060|### Requirements Analysis\\n061|- The application must be a standalone .exe built with Python (e.g., using PyInstaller).\\n062|- It must integrate with the GPT-4 API for generating test input Excel files.\\n063|- The system should analyze output files, categorize errors, and log all data into a RAG knowledge base.\\n064|- The application must continuously improve input generation based on historical data and error analysis.\\n065|- Multi-client support is required, with data isolation and secure access for each client.\\n066|- Local storage must be implemented using either SQLite or JSON, configurable per deployment.\\n067|- The UI should be simple, allowing users to upload/download files, view error reports, and manage clients.\\n068|\\n069|### Requirements Pool\\n070|- **P0 (Must-have):**\\n071|  - Standalone .exe application\\n072|  - GPT-4 API integration for input generation\\n073|  - Output file analysis and error categorization\\n074|  - RAG knowledge base for logging and retrieval\\n075|  - Multi-client support with data isolation\\n076|  - Local storage (SQLite/JSON)\\n077|- **P1 (Should-have):**\\n078|  - Configurable error categories\\n079|  - User management and access control\\n080|  - Dashboard for error trends and input improvements\\n081|- **P2 (Nice-to-have):**\\n082|  - Customizable test scenario templates\\n083|  - Integration with external test management tools\\n084|  - Advanced analytics and reporting\\n085|\\n086|### UI Design Draft\\n087|- **Main Dashboard:**\\n088|  - Client selector (dropdown)\\n089|  - Upload input/output Excel files\\n090|  - Generate new test input (button)\\n091|  - View error reports (table/list)\\n092|  - Knowledge base access (search/view)\\n093|- **Settings:**\\n094|  - Storage type selection (SQLite/JSON)\\n095|  - API key management\\n096|  - User management (if enabled)\\n097|\\n098|### Open Questions\\n099|- What is the expected volume of test scenarios and clients?\\n100|- Are there specific security or compliance requirements for local storage?\\n101|- Should the RAG knowledge base support external integrations?\\n102|- What level of customization is needed for error categories and reporting?\\n103|- Is there a preferred framework for the UI (Tkinter, PyQt, etc.)?\\n\'\n\nCommand Editor.read executed: file_path=\'/home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/python_gpt4_test_automation_app/docs/system_design.md\' block_content="001|## Implementation approach\\n002|\\n003|We will build a Python desktop application packaged as a standalone .exe using PyInstaller. The app will use Tkinter for the UI (simple, cross-platform, and compatible with .exe builds). For GPT-4 API integration, we\'ll use the requests library. Excel file generation and analysis will be handled by openpyxl and pandas. Error categorization logic will be modular and configurable. The RAG knowledge base will use SQLite (default) or JSON for local storage, with per-client data isolation. Multi-client support will be managed via user authentication and client-specific data partitions. The system will continuously improve input generation by leveraging historical data and error logs stored in the knowledge base.\\n004|\\n005|## File list\\n006|\\n007|- main.py\\n008|- ui.py\\n009|- gpt4_api.py\\n010|- excel_manager.py\\n011|- error_categorizer.py\\n012|- rag_knowledge_base.py\\n013|- client_manager.py\\n014|- storage.py\\n015|- config.py\\n016|- requirements.txt\\n017|- interfaces:\\n023|\\n024|```mermaid\\n025|classDiagram\\n026|    class Main {\\n027|        <<entry point>>\\n028|        +run()\\n029|    }\\n030|    class UI {\\n031|        +start()\\n032|        +show_dashboard()\\n033|        +upload_file(client_id: str)\\n034|        +download_file(client_id: str)\\n035|        +show_error_report(client_id: str)\\n036|        +select_client(client_id: str)\\n037|    }\\n038|    class GPT4API {\\n039|        +generate_test_input(prompt: str) -> dict\\n040|    }\\n041|    class ExcelManager {\\n042|        +create_input_excel(data: dict, path: str)\\n043|        +analyze_output_excel(path: str) -> dict\\n044|    }\\n045|    class ErrorCategorizer {\\n046|        +categorize_errors(output_data: dict) -> list\\n047|        +configurable_categories: dict\\n048|    }\\n049|    class RAGKnowledgeBase {\\n050|        +log_test_data(client_id: str, input_data: dict, output_data: dict, errors: list)\\n051|        +retrieve_similar_cases(query: str, client_id: str) -> list\\n052|        +improve_input_generation(client_id: str) -> dict\\n053|    }\\n054|    class ClientManager {\\n055|        +authenticate(username: str, password: str) -> str\\n056|        +get_clients() -> list\\n057|        +add_client(client_info: dict)\\n058|    }\\n059|    class Storage {\\n060|        +save_data(client_id: str, data: dict)\\n061|        +load_data(client_id: str) -> dict\\n062|        +set_storage_type(type: str)\\n063|    }\\n064|    Main --> UI\\n065|    Main --> ClientManager\\n066|    Main --> Storage\\n067|    UI --> ExcelManager\\n068|    UI --> ErrorCategorizer\\n069|    UI --> RAGKnowledgeBase\\n070|    UI --> GPT4API\\n071|    ExcelManager --> ErrorCategorizer\\n072|    ErrorCategorizer --> RAGKnowledgeBase\\n073|    RAGKnowledgeBase --> Storage\\n074|    ClientManager --> Storage\\n075|```\\n076|\\n077|## Program call flow:\\n078|\\n079|```mermaid\\n080|sequenceDiagram\\n081|    participant M as Main\\n082|    participant U as UI\\n083|    participant CM as ClientManager\\n084|    participant S as Storage\\n085|    participant G as GPT4API\\n086|    participant EM as ExcelManager\\n087|    participant EC as ErrorCategorizer\\n088|    participant KB as RAGKnowledgeBase\\n089|\\n090|    M->>U: start()\\n091|    U->>CM: authenticate(username, password)\\n092|    CM->>S: load_data(client_id)\\n093|    U->>G: generate_test_input(prompt)\\n094|    G-->>U: return test input data\\n095|    U->>EM: create_input_excel(data, path)\\n096|    U->>EM: analyze_output_excel(path)\\n097|    EM-->>U: return output data\\n098|    U->>EC: categorize_errors(output_data)\\n099|    EC-->>U: return error list\\n100|    U->>KB: log_test_data(client_id, input_data, output_data, errors)\\n101|    U->>KB: improve_input_generation(client_id)\\n102|    KB-->>U: return improved input suggestions\\n103|    U->>S: save_data(client_id, all_data)\\n104|    U->>U: show_error_report(client_id)\\n105|\\n106|## Anything UNCLEAR\\n107|\\n108|- Expected volume of test scenarios and clients is not specified; may import tkinter as tk
from tkinter import ttk, filedialog, messagebox
import os
from excel_manager import ExcelManager
from error_categorizer import ErrorCategorizer
from rag_knowledge_base import RAGKnowledgeBase
from gpt4_api import GPT4API

class UI:
    """
    Tkinter-based user interface for python_gpt4_test_automation_app.
    Provides dashboard, file upload/download, error report viewing, and client selection.
    """

    def __init__(self, root, client_manager, storage, config):
        self.root = root
        self.client_manager = client_manager
        self.storage = storage
        self.config = config

        self.gpt4_api = GPT4API(self.config.get_gpt4_api_key())
        self.excel_manager = ExcelManager()
        self.error_categorizer = ErrorCategorizer()
        self.rag_kb = RAGKnowledgeBase(self.storage)

        self.current_client_id = None
        self.input_data = None
        self.output_data = None
        self.error_list = None

        self.root.title("GPT-4 Test Automation App")
        self.root.geometry("900x600")
        self._build_login_screen()

    def start(self):
        self.root.deiconify()

    def _build_login_screen(self):
        self._clear_root()
        self.login_frame = ttk.Frame(self.root, padding=30)
        self.login_frame.pack(expand=True)

        ttk.Label(self.login_frame, text="Login", font=("Arial", 18)).grid(row=0, column=0, columnspan=2, pady=10)
        ttk.Label(self.login_frame, text="Username:").grid(row=1, column=0, sticky="e", pady=5)
        self.username_entry = ttk.Entry(self.login_frame)
        self.username_entry.grid(row=1, column=1, pady=5)
        ttk.Label(self.login_frame, text="Password:").grid(row=2, column=0, sticky="e", pady=5)
        self.password_entry = ttk.Entry(self.login_frame, show="*")
        self.password_entry.grid(row=2, column=1, pady=5)

        self.login_btn = ttk.Button(self.login_frame, text="Login", command=self._login)
        self.login_btn.grid(row=3, column=0, columnspan=2, pady=15)

    def _login(self):
        username = self.username_entry.get()
        password = self.password_entry.get()
        client_id = self.client_manager.authenticate(username, password)
        if client_id:
            self.current_client_id = client_id
            self._build_dashboard()
        else:
            messagebox.showerror("Login Failed", "Invalid username or password.")

    def _build_dashboard(self):
        self._clear_root()
        self.dashboard_frame = ttk.Frame(self.root, padding=10)
        self.dashboard_frame.pack(fill="both", expand=True)

        # Client selector
        ttk.Label(self.dashboard_frame, text="Select Client:", font=("Arial", 12)).grid(row=0, column=0, sticky="w")
        self.client_selector = ttk.Combobox(self.dashboard_frame, values=self.client_manager.get_clients(), state="readonly")
        self.client_selector.set(self.current_client_id)
        self.client_selector.grid(row=0, column=1, sticky="w")
        self.client_selector.bind("<<ComboboxSelected>>", self._on_client_selected)

        # File upload/download
        ttk.Label(self.dashboard_frame, text="Input Excel File:").grid(row=1, column=0, sticky="w", pady=5)
        self.upload_input_btn = ttk.Button(self.dashboard_frame, text="Upload", command=self.upload_file)
        self.upload_input_btn.grid(row=1, column=1, sticky="w", pady=5)

        ttk.Label(self.dashboard_frame, text="Output Excel File:").grid(row=2, column=0, sticky="w", pady=5)
        self.upload_output_btn = ttk.Button(self.dashboard_frame, text="Upload", command=self.upload_output_file)
        self.upload_output_btn.grid(row=2, column=1, sticky="w", pady=5)

        self.download_btn = ttk.Button(self.dashboard_frame, text="Download Last Input", command=self.download_file)
        self.download_btn.grid(row=3, column=1, sticky="w", pady=5)

        # Generate new test input
        self.generate_btn = ttk.Button(self.dashboard_frame, text="Generate New Test Input", command=self.generate_test_input)
        self.generate_btn.grid(row=4, column=0, columnspan=2, pady=10)

        # Error report
        self.error_report_btn = ttk.Button(self.dashboard_frame, text="View Error Report", command=self.show_error_report)
        self.error_report_btn.grid(row=5, column=0, columnspan=2, pady=10)

        # Knowledge base access
        ttk.Label(self.dashboard_frame, text="Knowledge Base Query:").grid(row=6, column=0, sticky="w", pady=5)
        self.kb_query_entry = ttk.Entry(self.dashboard_frame, width=40)
        self.kb_query_entry.grid(row=6, column=1, sticky="w", pady=5)
        self.kb_search_btn = ttk.Button(self.dashboard_frame, text="Search KB", command=self.search_knowledge_base)
        self.kb_search_btn.grid(row=7, column=1, sticky="w", pady=5)

        # Improved input suggestion
        self.improve_input_btn = ttk.Button(self.dashboard_frame, text="Suggest Improved Input", command=self.suggest_improved_input)
        self.improve_input_btn.grid(row=8, column=0, columnspan=2, pady=10)

        # Status
        self.status_label = ttk.Label(self.dashboard_frame, text="", foreground="blue")
        self.status_label.grid(row=9, column=0, columnspan=2, pady=10)

        # Error report table
        self.error_report_tree = None

    def _on_client_selected(self, event):
        self.current_client_id = self.client_selector.get()
        self.status_label.config(text=f"Switched to client: {self.current_client_id}")

    def upload_file(self):
        file_path = filedialog.askopenfilename(
            title="Select Input Excel File",
            filetypes=[("Excel Files", "*.xlsx *.xls")]
        )
        if file_path:
            try:
                self.input_data = self.excel_manager.analyze_output_excel(file_path)
                self.storage.save_data(self.current_client_id, {"input_data": self.input_data})
                self.status_label.config(text=f"Input file uploaded and analyzed for client {self.current_client_id}.")
            except Exception as e:
                messagebox.showerror("Upload Error", f"Failed to upload input file: {str(e)}")

    def upload_output_file(self):
        file_path = filedialog.askopenfilename(
            title="Select Output Excel File",
            filetypes=[("Excel Files", "*.xlsx *.xls")]
        )
        if file_path:
            try:
                self.output_data = self.excel_manager.analyze_output_excel(file_path)
                self.storage.save_data(self.current_client_id, {"output_data": self.output_data})
                self.status_label.config(text=f"Output file uploaded and analyzed for client {self.current_client_id}.")
            except Exception as e:
                messagebox.showerror("Upload Error", f"Failed to upload output file: {str(e)}")

    def download_file(self):
        data = self.storage.load_data(self.current_client_id)
        if data and "input_data" in data:
            save_path = filedialog.asksaveasfilename(
                title="Save Input Excel File",
                defaultextension=".xlsx",
                filetypes=[("Excel Files", "*.xlsx *.xls")]
            )
            if save_path:
                try:
                    self.excel_manager.create_input_excel(data["input_data"], save_path)
                    self.status_label.config(text=f"Input file downloaded for client {self.current_client_id}.")
                except Exception as e:
                    messagebox.showerror("Download Error", f"Failed to download input file: {str(e)}")
        else:
            messagebox.showinfo("No Data", "No input data available for download.")

    def generate_test_input(self):
        prompt = "Generate test input for automated test scenarios."
        try:
            test_input = self.gpt4_api.generate_test_input(prompt)
            save_path = filedialog.asksaveasfilename(
                title="Save Generated Input Excel File",
                defaultextension=".xlsx",
                filetypes=[("Excel Files", "*.xlsx *.xls")]
            )
            if save_path:
                self.excel_manager.create_input_excel(test_input, save_path)
                self.storage.save_data(self.current_client_id, {"input_data": test_input})
                self.status_label.config(text=f"Test input generated and saved for client {self.current_client_id}.")
        except Exception as e:
            messagebox.showerror("Generation Error", f"Failed to generate test input: {str(e)}")

    def show_error_report(self):
        data = self.storage.load_data(self.current_client_id)
        if data and "output_data" in data:
            try:
                self.error_list = self.error_categorizer.categorize_errors(data["output_data"])
                self.rag_kb.log_test_data(self.current_client_id, data.get("input_data", {}), data["output_data"], self.error_list)
                self._show_error_report_table(self.error_list)
                self.status_label.config(text=f"Error report generated for client {self.current_client_id}.")
            except Exception as e:
                messagebox.showerror("Error Report", f"Failed to generate error report: {str(e)}")
        else:
            messagebox.showinfo("No Data", "No output data available for error report.")

    def _show_error_report_table(self, error_list):
        if self.error_report_tree:
            self.error_report_tree.destroy()
        self.error_report_tree = ttk.Treeview(self.dashboard_frame, columns=("Category", "Description"), show="headings", height=10)
        self.error_report_tree.heading("Category", text="Category")
        self.error_report_tree.heading("Description", text="Description")
        self.error_report_tree.grid(row=10, column=0, columnspan=2, pady=10, sticky="nsew")
        for error in error_list:
            self.error_report_tree.insert("", "end", values=(error.get("category", ""), error.get("description", "")))

    def search_knowledge_base(self):
        query = self.kb_query_entry.get()
        if not query:
            messagebox.showinfo("No Query", "Please enter a query to search the knowledge base.")
            return
        try:
            results = self.rag_kb.retrieve_similar_cases(query, self.current_client_id)
            result_str = "\n".join([str(r) for r in results]) if results else "No similar cases found."
            messagebox.showinfo("Knowledge Base Results", result_str)
        except Exception as e:
            messagebox.showerror("Knowledge Base Error", f"Failed to search knowledge base: {str(e)}")

    def suggest_improved_input(self):
        try:
            improved_input = self.rag_kb.improve_input_generation(self.current_client_id)
            messagebox.showinfo("Improved Input Suggestion", str(improved_input))
        except Exception as e:
            messagebox.showerror("Improvement Error", f"Failed to suggest improved input: {str(e)}")

    def _clear_root(self):
        for widget in self.root.winfo_children():
            widget.destroy()

import requests
import json

class GPT4API:
    """
    Handles GPT-4 API integration for generating test input data.
    Provides generate_test_input(prompt: str) -> dict method.
    """

    def __init__(self, api_key: str, api_url: str = "https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions"):
        self.api_key = api_key
        self.api_url = api_url
        self.model = "gpt-4"  # You may change this if using a different model

    def generate_test_input(self, prompt: str) -> dict:
        """
        Sends a prompt to the GPT-4 API and returns the generated test input as a dictionary.
        The response is expected to be a JSON string representing the test input data.
        """
        headers = {
            "Authorization": f"Bearer {self.api_key}",
            "Content-Type": "application/json"
        }
        payload = {
            "model": self.model,
            "messages": [
                {"role": "system", "content": "You are a helpful assistant that generates test input data for automated test scenarios in JSON format."},
                {"role": "user", "content": prompt}
            ],
            "temperature": 0.2,
            "max_tokens": 2048
        }

        response = requests.post(self.api_url, headers=headers, data=json.dumps(payload))
        if response.status_code != 200:
            raise Exception(f"GPT-4 API request failed: {response.status_code} {response.text}")

        result = response.json()
        # Extract the content from the response
        try:
            content = result["choices"][0]["message"]["content"]
            # Try to parse the content as JSON
            test_input_data = json.loads(content)
            if not isinstance(test_input_data, dict):
                raise Exception("GPT-4 response is not a valid JSON object.")
            return test_input_data
        except (KeyError, IndexError, json.JSONDecodeError) as e:
            raise Exception(f"Failed to parse GPT-4 response: {str(e)}")

You may change this if using a different model\n\n    def generate_test_input(self, prompt: str) -> dict:\n        """\n        Sends a prompt to the GPT-4 API and returns the generated test input as a dictionary.\n        The response is expected to be a JSON string representing the test input data.\n        """\n        headers = {\n            "Authorization": f"Bearer {self.api_key}",\n            "Content-Type": "application/json"\n        }\n        payload = {\n            "model": self.model,\n            "messages": [\n                {"role": "system", "content": "You are a helpful assistant that generates test input data for automated test scenarios in JSON format."},\n                {"role": "user", "content": prompt}\n            ],\n            "temperature": 0.2,\n            "max_tokens": 2048\n        }\n\n        response = requests.post(self.api_url, headers=headers, data=json.dumps(payload))\n        if response.status_code != 200:\n            raise Exception(f"GPT-4 API request failed: {response.status_code} {response.text}")\n\n        result = response.json()\n        # Extract the content from the response\n        try:\n            content = result["choices"][0]["message"]["content"]\n            # Try to parse the content as JSON\n            test_input_data = json.loads(content)\n            if not isinstance(test_input_data, dict):\n                raise Exception("GPT-4 response is not a valid JSON object.")\n            return test_input_data\n        except (KeyError, IndexError, json.JSONDecodeError) as e:\n            raise Exception(f"Failed to parse GPT-4 response: {str(e)}")'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of gpt4_api.py has been completed according to the system design, providing the GPT4API class and the generate_test_input method. Technical Specifications\\n059|\\n060|### Requirements Analysis\\n061|- The application must be a standalone .exe built with Python (e.g., using PyInstaller).\\n062|- It must integrate with the GPT-4 API for generating test input Excel files.\\n063|- The system should analyze output files, categorize errors, and log all data into a RAG knowledge base.\\n064|- The application must continuously improve input generation based on historical data and error analysis.\\n065|- Multi-client support is required, with data isolation and secure access for each client.\\n066|- Local storage must be implemented using either SQLite or JSON, configurable per deployment.\\n067|- The UI should be simple, allowing users to upload/download files, view error reports, and manage clients.\\n068|\\n069|### Requirements Pool\\n070|- **P0 (Must-have):**\\n071|  - Standalone .exe application\\n072|  - GPT-4 API integration for input generation\\n073|  - Output file analysis and error categorization\\n074|  - RAG knowledge base for logging and retrieval\\n075|  - Multi-client support with data isolation\\n076|  - Local storage (SQLite/JSON)\\n077|- **P1 (Should-have):**\\n078|  - Configurable error categories\\n079|  - User management and access control\\n080|  - Dashboard for error trends and input improvements\\n081|- **P2 (Nice-to-have):**\\n082|  - Customizable test scenario templates\\n083|  - Integration with external test management tools\\n084|  - Advanced analytics and reporting\\n085|\\n086|### UI Design Draft\\n087|- **Main Dashboard:**\\n088|  - The app will use Tkinter for the UI (simple, cross-platform, and compatible with .exe builds). For GPT-4 API integration, we\'ll use the requests library. Excel file generation and analysis will be handled by openpyxl and pandas. Error categorization logic will be modular and configurable. The RAG knowledge base will use SQLite (default) or JSON for local storage, with per-client data isolation. Multi-client support will be managed via user authentication and client-specific data partitions. The system will continuously improve input generation by leveraging historical data and error logs stored in the knowledge base.\\n004|\\n005|## File list\\n006|\\n007|- main.py\\n008|- ui.py\\n009|- gpt4_api.py\\n010|- excel_manager.py\\n011|- error_categorizer.py\\n012|- rag_knowledge_base.py\\n013|- client_manager.py\\n014|- storage.py\\n015|- config.py\\n016|- requirements.txt\\n017|- interfaces:\\n023|\\n024|```mermaid\\n025|classDiagram\\n026|    class Main {\\n027|        <<entry point>>\\n028|        +run()\\n029|    }\\n030|    class UI {\\n031|        +start()\\n032|        +show_dashboard()\\n033|        +upload_file(client_id: str)\\n034|        +download_file(client_id: str)\\n035|        +show_error_report(client_id: str)\\n036|        +select_client(client_id: str)\\n043|        +analyze_output_excel(path: str) -> dict\\n044|    }\\n045|    class ErrorCategorizer {\\n046|        +categorize_errors(output_data: dict) -> list\\n047|        +configurable_categories: dict\\n048|    }\\n049|    class RAGKnowledgeBase {\\n050|        +log_test_data(client_id: str, input_data: dict, output_data: dict, errors: list)\\n051|        +retrieve_similar_cases(query: str, client_id: str) -> list\\n052|        +improve_input_generation(client_id: str) -> dict\\n053|    }\\n054|    class ClientManager {\\n055|        +authenticate(username: str, password: str) -> str\\n056|        +get_clients() -> list\\n057|        +add_client(client_info: dict)\\n058|    }\\n059|    class Storage {\\n060|        +save_data(client_id: str, data: dict)\\n061|        +load_data(client_id: str) -> dict\\n062|        +set_storage_type(type: str)\\n063|    }\\n064|    Main --> UI\\n065|    Main --> flow:\\n078|\\n079|```mermaid\\n080|sequenceDiagram\\n081|    participant M as Main\\n082|    participant U as UI\\n083|    participant CM as ClientManager\\n084|    participant S as Storage\\n085| self.status_label.config(text=f"Output file uploaded and analyzed for client {self.current_client_id}.")\n            except Exception as e:\n                messagebox.showerror("Upload Error", save_path = filedialog.asksaveasfilename(\n                title="Save Input Excel File",\n                defaultextension=".xlsx",\n                filetypes=[("Excel Files", "*.xlsx *.xls")]\n            )\n            if save_path:\n                try:\n                    self.excel_manager.create_input_excel(data["input_data"], save_path)\n                    self.status_label.config(text=f"Input file downloaded for client {self.current_client_id}.")\n                except Exception as e:\n                    messagebox.showerror("Download Error", f"Failed to download input file: {str(e)}")\n        else:\n            messagebox.showinfo("No Data", "No input data available for download.")\n\n    def generate_test_input(self):\n        prompt = "Generate test input for automated test scenarios."\n        try:\n            test_input = self.gpt4_api.generate_test_input(prompt)\n            save_path = filedialog.asksaveasfilename(\n                title="Save Generated Input Excel File",\n                defaultextension=".xlsx",\n                filetypes=[("Excel Files", "*.xlsx *.xls")]\n            )\n            if save_path:\n                self.excel_manager.create_input_excel(test_input, save_path)\n                self.storage.save_data(self.current_client_id, {"input_data": test_input})\n                self.status_label.config(text=f"Test input generated and saved for client {self.current_client_id}.")\n        except Exception as e:\n            messagebox.showerror("Generation Error", f"Failed to generate test input: {str(e)}")\n\n    def show_error_report(self):\n        data = self.storage.load_data(self.current_client_id)\n        if data and "output_data" in data:\n            try:\n                self.error_list = self.error_categorizer.categorize_errors(data["output_data"])\n                self.rag_kb.log_test_data(self.current_client_id, data.get("input_data", {}), import openpyxl
import pandas as pd
from typing import Dict, Any

class ExcelManager:
    """
    Handles creation and analysis of Excel files for input and output test scenarios.
    Uses openpyxl for writing and pandas for reading/analyzing.
    """

    def create_input_excel(self, data: Dict[str, Any], path: str):
        """
        Creates an Excel file at the given path from the provided data dictionary.
        The data dict should be structured as {sheet_name: [row_dict, ...], ...}
        Each row_dict is a mapping of column_name to value.
        """
        wb = openpyxl.Workbook()
        # Remove the default sheet
        default_sheet = wb.active
        wb.remove(default_sheet)

        for sheet_name, rows in data.items():
            ws = wb.create_sheet(title=sheet_name)
            if not rows:
                continue
            # Get all column names from the first row
            columns = list(rows[0].keys())
            ws.append(columns)
            for row in rows:
                ws.append([row.get(col, "") for col in columns])
        wb.save(path)

    def analyze_output_excel(self, path: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """
        Analyzes the Excel file at the given path and returns its contents as a dictionary.
        The returned dict is structured as {sheet_name: [row_dict, ...], ...}
        Each row_dict is a mapping of column_name to value.
        """
        xls = pd.ExcelFile(path)
        result = {}
        for sheet_name in xls.sheet_names:
            df = pd.read_excel(xls, sheet_name=sheet_name)
            # Convert DataFrame to list of dicts
            rows = df.to_dict(orient="records")
            result[sheet_name] = rows
        return result

multiple clients so that different teams can use it independently.\\n027|- As a product owner, I want local storage using SQLite or JSON so that data is secure and easily accessible.\\n028|### Competitive Analysis\\n029|\\n030|| Product/Tool                | Pros                                              | Cons                                              |\\n031||-----------------------------|---------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------|\\n032|| TestRail                    | Robust test management, multi-client support      | No GPT-4 integration, limited automation          |\\n033|| Selenium                    | Widely used, open-source, flexible                | Manual test case creation, no built-in RAG        |\\n034|| Applitools                  | Visual testing, AI-powered error detection        | Focused on UI, not Excel or GPT-4                 |\\n035|| Katalon Studio              | All-in-one automation, supports data-driven tests | No GPT-4, limited error categorization            |\\n036|| Tricentis Tosca             | Model-based, strong analytics                     | Expensive, complex setup                          |\\n037|| Testim                      | AI-powered, easy integration                      | Limited local storage, no RAG knowledge base      |\\n038|| Custom Python Frameworks    | Highly customizable, open-source                  | Requires manual setup, lacks advanced analytics   |\\n039|\\n040|### Competitive Quadrant Chart\\n041|```mermaid\\n042|quadrantChart\\n043|    title "Automated Test Scenario Tools: Innovation vs. Integration"\\n044|    x-axis "Low Innovation" --> "High Innovation"\\n045|    y-axis "Low Integration" --> "High Integration"\\n046|    quadrant-1 "Expand Features"\\n047|    quadrant-2 "Promote Integration"\\n048|    quadrant-3 "Re-evaluate"\\n049|    quadrant-4 "Improve Innovation"\\n050|    "TestRail": [0.3, 0.8]\\n051|    "Selenium": [0.4, 0.6]\\n052|    "Applitools": [0.7, 0.5]\\n053|    "Katalon Studio": [0.5, 0.7]\\n054|    "Tricentis Tosca": [0.6, 0.9]\\n055|    "Testim": [0.8, 0.4]\\n056|    "python_gpt4_test_automation_app": [0.9, 0.8]\\n057|```\\n058|## 3. Technical Specifications\\n059|\\n060|### Requirements Analysis\\n061|- The application must be a standalone .exe built with Python (e.g., using PyInstaller).\\n062|- It must integrate with the GPT-4 API for generating test input Excel files.\\n063|- The system should analyze output files, categorize errors, and log all data into a RAG knowledge base.\\n064|- The application must continuously improve input generation based on historical data and error analysis.\\n065|- Multi-client support is required, with data isolation and secure access for each client.\\n066|- Local storage must be implemented using either SQLite or JSON, configurable per deployment.\\n067|- The UI should be simple, allowing users to upload/download files, view error reports, and manage clients.\\n068|\\n069|### Requirements Pool\\n070|- **P0 (Must-have):**\\n071|  - Standalone .exe application\\n072|  - GPT-4 API integration for input generation\\n073|  - Output file analysis and error categorization\\n074|  - RAG knowledge base for logging and retrieval\\n075|  - Multi-client support with data isolation\\n076|  - Local storage (SQLite/JSON)\\n077|- **P1 (Should-have):**\\n078|  - Configurable error categories\\n079|  - User management and access control\\n080|  - Dashboard for error trends and input improvements\\n081|- **P2 (Nice-to-have):**\\n082|  - Customizable test scenario templates\\n083|  - Integration with external test management tools\\n084|  - Advanced analytics and reporting\\n085|\\n086|### UI Design Draft\\n087|- **Main Dashboard:**\\n088|  - The app will use Tkinter for the UI (simple, cross-platform, and compatible with .exe builds). For GPT-4 API integration, we\'ll use the requests library. Excel file generation and analysis will be handled by openpyxl and pandas. Error categorization logic will be modular and configurable. The RAG knowledge base will use SQLite (default) or JSON for local storage, with per-client data isolation. Multi-client support will be managed via user authentication and client-specific data partitions. The system will continuously improve input generation by leveraging historical data and error logs stored in the knowledge base.\\n004|\\n005|## File list\\n006|\\n007|- main.py\\n008|- ui.py\\n009|- gpt4_api.py\\n010|- excel_manager.py\\n011|- error_categorizer.py\\n012|- rag_knowledge_base.py\\n013|- client_manager.py\\n014|- storage.py\\n015|- config.py\\n016|- requirements.txt\\n017|- +show_dashboard()\\n033|        +upload_file(client_id: str)\\n034|        +download_file(client_id: str)\\n035|        +show_error_report(client_id: str)\\n036|        +select_client(client_id: +retrieve_similar_cases(query: str, client_id: str) -> list\\n052|        +improve_input_generation(client_id: str) -> dict\\n053|    }\\n054|    class ClientManager {\\n055|        +authenticate(username: str, password: str) -> str\\n056|        +get_clients() -> list\\n057|        +add_client(client_info: dict)\\n058|    }\\n059|    class Storage {\\n060|        +save_data(client_id: str, data: dict)\\n061|        +load_data(client_id: str) -> dict\\n062|        +set_storage_type(type: str)\\n063|    }\\n064|    Main --> UI\\n065|    Main --> upload_file(self):\n        file_path = filedialog.askopenfilename(\n            title="Select Input Excel File",\n            filetypes=[("Excel Files", "*.xlsx *.xls")]\n        )\n        if self.excel_manager.analyze_output_excel(file_path)\n                self.storage.save_data(self.current_client_id, {"output_data": self.output_data})\n                self.status_label.config(text=f"Output file uploaded and analyzed for client {self.current_client_id}.")\n            except Exception as e:\n                messagebox.showerror("Upload Error", f"Failed to upload output file: {str(e)}")\n\n    def download_file(self):\n        data = self.storage.load_data(self.current_client_id)\n        if data and "input_data" in data:\n            save_path = filedialog.asksaveasfilename(\n                title="Save Input Excel File",\n                defaultextension=".xlsx",\n                filetypes=[("Excel Files", "*.xlsx *.xls")]\n            )\n            if save_path:\n                try:\n                    self.excel_manager.create_input_excel(data["input_data"], save_path)\n You may change this if using a different model\n\n    def generate_test_input(self, prompt: str) -> dict:\n        """\n        Sends a prompt to the GPT-4 API and returns the generated test input as a dictionary.\n        The response is expected to be a JSON string representing the test input data.\n        """\n        headers = {\n            "Authorization": f"Bearer {self.api_key}",\n            "Content-Type": "application/json"\n        }\n        payload = {\n            "model": self.model,\n            "messages": [\n                {"role": "system", "content": "You are a helpful assistant that generates test input data for automated test scenarios in JSON format."},\n                {"role": "user", "content": prompt}\n            ],\n            "temperature": 0.2,\n            "max_tokens": 2048\n        }\n\n        response = requests.post(self.api_url, headers=headers, data=json.dumps(payload))\n        if response.status_code != 200:\n            raise Exception(f"GPT-4 API request failed: {response.status_code} {response.text}")\n\n        result = response.json()\n        # Extract the content from the response\n        try:\n            content = result["choices"][0]["message"]["content"]\n            # Try to parse the content as JSON\n            test_input_data = json.loads(content)\n            if not isinstance(test_input_data, dict):\n                raise Exception("GPT-4 response is not a valid JSON object.")\n            return test_input_data\n        except (KeyError, IndexError, json.JSONDecodeError) as e:\n            raise Exception(f"Failed to parse GPT-4 response: {str(e)}")'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of gpt4_api.py has been completed according to the system design, providing the GPT4API class and the generate_test_input method. Remove the default sheet\n        default_sheet = wb.active\n        wb.remove(default_sheet)\n\n        for sheet_name, rows in data.items():\n            ws = wb.create_sheet(title=sheet_name)\n            if not rows:\n                continue\n            # Get all column names from the first row\n            columns = list(rows[0].keys())\n            ws.append(columns)\n            for row in rows:\n                ws.append([row.get(col, "") for col in columns])\n        wb.save(path)\n\n    def analyze_output_excel(self, path: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        """\n        Analyzes the Excel file at the given path and returns its contents as a dictionary.\n        The returned dict is structured as {sheet_name: [row_dict, ...], ...}\n        Each row_dict is a mapping of column_name to value.\n        """\n        xls = pd.ExcelFile(path)\n        result = {}\n        for sheet_name in xls.sheet_names:\n            df = pd.read_excel(xls, sheet_name=sheet_name)\n            # Convert DataFrame to list of dicts\n            rows = df.to_dict(orient="records")\n            result[sheet_name] = rows\n        return result'}, {'role': 'user', 'content': '\n# Past Experience\n<experience>\n\n# Tool State\nThe current editor state is:\n(Current directory: /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace)\n(Open file: None)\n\n# Current Plan\n[GOAL] [Message] from Mike to Alex: Please implement the Python standalone .exe application as per the system design. from typing import Dict, List, Any

class ErrorCategorizer:
    """
    Handles error categorization logic for analyzed output data.
    Provides categorize_errors(output_data: dict) -> list and configurable_categories: dict.
    """

    def __init__(self, categories: Dict[str, str] = None):
        # Default error categories can be customized
        self.configurable_categories = categories if categories else {
            "MissingValue": "A required value is missing.",
            "TypeMismatch": "Data type does not match expected type.",
            "OutOfRange": "Value is out of allowed range.",
            "FormatError": "Value format is incorrect.",
            "LogicError": "Logical inconsistency detected.",
            "UnknownError": "Uncategorized error."
        }

    def categorize_errors(self, output_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> List[Dict[str, str]]:
        """
        Analyzes output_data and returns a list of categorized errors.
        Each error is a dict: {"category": str, "description": str}
        """
        errors = []
        for sheet_name, rows in output_data.items():
            for row_idx, row in enumerate(rows):
                for col, value in row.items():
                    # Example error checks (can be extended/configured)
                    if value is None or (isinstance(value, str) and value.strip() == ""):
                        errors.append({
                            "category": "MissingValue",
                            "description": f"Sheet '{sheet_name}', Row {row_idx+2}, Column '{col}': {self.configurable_categories['MissingValue']}"
                        })
                    elif isinstance(value, str) and value.lower() == "error":
                        errors.append({
                            "category": "LogicError",
                            "description": f"Sheet '{sheet_name}', Row {row_idx+2}, Column '{col}': {self.configurable_categories['LogicError']}"
                        })
                    elif isinstance(value, str) and not self._is_valid_format(value):
                        errors.append({
                            "category": "FormatError",
                            "description": f"Sheet '{sheet_name}', Row {row_idx+2}, Column '{col}': {self.configurable_categories['FormatError']}"
                        })
                    elif self._is_out_of_range(value):
                        errors.append({
                            "category": "OutOfRange",
                            "description": f"Sheet '{sheet_name}', Row {row_idx+2}, Column '{col}': {self.configurable_categories['OutOfRange']}"
                        })
                    # Add more error checks as needed

        if not errors:
            errors.append({
                "category": "UnknownError",
                "description": "No errors detected or unable to categorize errors."
            })
        return errors

    def _is_valid_format(self, value: str) -> bool:
        # Example: check if value is a valid number or date string
        # Extend this function for more format checks
        try:
            float(value)
            return True
        except ValueError:
            pass
        # Check for date format (YYYY-MM-DD)
        import re
        if re.match(r"^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$", value):
            return True
        return False

    def _is_out_of_range(self, value: Any) -> bool:
        # Example: check if numeric value is out of range
        if isinstance(value, (int, float)):
            if value < 0 or value > 1e6:  # Example range
                return True
        return False

The app will use Tkinter for the UI (simple, cross-platform, and compatible with .exe builds). For GPT-4 API integration, we\'ll use the requests library. Excel file generation and analysis will be handled by openpyxl and pandas. Error categorization logic will be modular and configurable. The RAG knowledge base will use SQLite (default) or JSON for local storage, with per-client data isolation. Multi-client support will be managed via user authentication and client-specific data partitions. The system will continuously improve input generation by leveraging historical data and error logs stored in the knowledge base.\\n004|\\n005|## File list\\n006|\\n007|- main.py\\n008|- ui.py\\n009|- +show_dashboard()\\n033|        +upload_file(client_id: str)\\n034|        +download_file(client_id: str)\\n035|        +show_error_report(client_id: str)\\n036|        +select_client(client_id: +retrieve_similar_cases(query: str, client_id: str) -> list\\n052|        +improve_input_generation(client_id: str) -> dict\\n053|    }\\n054|    class ClientManager {\\n055|        +authenticate(username: str, password: str) -> str\\n056|        +get_clients() -> list\\n057|        +add_client(client_info: dict)\\n058|    }\\n059|    class Storage {\\n060|        +save_data(client_id: str, data: dict)\\n061|        +load_data(client_id: str) -> dict\\n062|        +set_storage_type(type: str)\\n063|    }\\n064|    Main --> UI\\n065|    Main --> flow:\\n078|\\n079|```mermaid\\n080|sequenceDiagram\\n081|    participant M as Main\\n082|    participant U as UI\\n083|    participant CM as ClientManager\\n084|    participant S as Storage\\n085| You may change this if using a different model\n\n    def generate_test_input(self, prompt: str) -> dict:\n        """\n        Sends a prompt to the GPT-4 API and returns the generated test input as a dictionary.\n        The response is expected to be a JSON string representing the test input data.\n        """\n        headers = {\n            "Authorization": f"Bearer {self.api_key}",\n            "Content-Type": "application/json"\n        }\n        payload = {\n            "model": self.model,\n            "messages": [\n                {"role": "system", "content": "You are a helpful assistant that generates test input data for automated test scenarios in JSON format."},\n                {"role": "user", "content": prompt}\n            ],\n            "temperature": 0.2,\n            "max_tokens": 2048\n        }\n\n        response = requests.post(self.api_url, headers=headers, data=json.dumps(payload))\n        if response.status_code != 200:\n            raise Exception(f"GPT-4 API request failed: {response.status_code} {response.text}")\n\n        result = response.json()\n        # Extract the content from the response\n        try:\n            content = result["choices"][0]["message"]["content"]\n            # Try to parse the content as JSON\n            test_input_data = json.loads(content)\n            if not isinstance(test_input_data, dict):\n                raise Exception("GPT-4 response is not a valid JSON object.")\n            return test_input_data\n        except (KeyError, IndexError, json.JSONDecodeError) as e:\n            raise Exception(f"Failed to parse GPT-4 response: {str(e)}")'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of gpt4_api.py has been completed according to the system design, providing the GPT4API class and the generate_test_input method. Remove the default sheet\n        default_sheet = wb.active\n        wb.remove(default_sheet)\n\n        for sheet_name, rows in data.items():\n            ws = wb.create_sheet(title=sheet_name)\n            if not rows:\n                continue\n            # Get all column names from the first row\n            columns = list(rows[0].keys())\n            ws.append(columns)\n            for row in rows:\n                ws.append([row.get(col, "") for col in columns])\n        wb.save(path)\n\n    def analyze_output_excel(self, path: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        """\n        Analyzes the Excel file at the given path and returns its contents as a dictionary.\n        The returned dict is structured as {sheet_name: [row_dict, ...], ...}\n        Each row_dict is a mapping of column_name to value.\n        """\n        xls = pd.ExcelFile(path)\n        result = {}\n        for sheet_name in xls.sheet_names:\n            df = pd.read_excel(xls, sheet_name=sheet_name)\n            # Convert DataFrame to list of dicts\n            rows = df.to_dict(orient="records")\n            result[sheet_name] = rows\n        return result'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of excel_manager.py is complete. Default error categories can be customized\n        self.configurable_categories = categories if categories else {\n            "MissingValue": "A required value is missing.",\n            "TypeMismatch": "Data type does not match expected type.",\n            "OutOfRange": "Value is out of allowed range.",\n            "FormatError": "Value format is incorrect.",\n            "LogicError": "Logical inconsistency detected.",\n            "UnknownError": "Uncategorized error."\n        }\n\n    def categorize_errors(self, output_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> List[Dict[str, str]]:\n        """\n        Analyzes output_data and returns a list of categorized errors.\n        Each error is a dict: {"category": str, "description": str}\n        """\n        errors = []\n        for sheet_name, rows in output_data.items():\n            for row_idx, row in enumerate(rows):\n                for col, value in row.items():\n                    # Example error checks (can be extended/configured)\n                    if value is None or (isinstance(value, str) and value.strip() == ""):\n                        errors.append({\n                            "category": "MissingValue",\n                            "description": f"Sheet \'{sheet_name}\', Row {row_idx+2}, Column \'{col}\': {self.configurable_categories[\'MissingValue\']}"\n                        })\n                    elif isinstance(value, str) and value.lower() == "error":\n                        errors.append({\n                            "category": "LogicError",\n                            "description": f"Sheet \'{sheet_name}\', Row {row_idx+2}, Column \'{col}\': {self.configurable_categories[\'LogicError\']}"\n                        })\n                    elif isinstance(value, str) and not self._is_valid_format(value):\n                        errors.append({\n                            "category": "FormatError",\n                            "description": f"Sheet \'{sheet_name}\', Row {row_idx+2}, Column \'{col}\': {self.configurable_categories[\'FormatError\']}"\n                        })\n                    elif self._is_out_of_range(value):\n                        errors.append({\n                            "category": "OutOfRange",\n                            "description": f"Sheet \'{sheet_name}\', Row {row_idx+2}, Column \'{col}\': {self.configurable_categories[\'OutOfRange\']}"\n                        })\n                    # The app will use Tkinter for the UI (simple, cross-platform, and compatible with .exe builds). For GPT-4 API integration, we\'ll use the requests library. Excel file generation and analysis will be handled by openpyxl and pandas. Error categorization logic will be modular and configurable. The RAG knowledge base will use SQLite (default) or JSON for local storage, with per-client data isolation. Multi-client support will be managed via user authentication and client-specific data partitions. The system will continuously improve input generation by leveraging historical data and error logs stored in the knowledge base.\\n004|\\n005|## File list\\n006|\\n007|- main.py\\n008|- ui.py\\n009|- +show_dashboard()\\n033|        +upload_file(client_id: str)\\n034|        +download_file(client_id: str)\\n035|        +show_error_report(client_id: str)\\n036|        +select_client(client_id: +retrieve_similar_cases(query: str, client_id: str) -> list\\n052|        +improve_input_generation(client_id: str) -> dict\\n053|    }\\n054|    class ClientManager {\\n055|        +authenticate(username: str, password: str) -> str\\n056|        +get_clients() -> list\\n057|        +add_client(client_info: dict)\\n058|    }\\n059|    class Storage {\\n060|        +save_data(client_id: str, data: dict)\\n061|        +load_data(client_id: str) -> dict\\n062|        +set_storage_type(type: str)\\n063|    }\\n064|    Main --> UI\\n065|    Main --> flow:\\n078|\\n079|```mermaid\\n080|sequenceDiagram\\n081|    participant M as Main\\n082|    participant U as UI\\n083|    participant CM as ClientManager\\n084|    participant S as Storage\\n085| Remove the default sheet\n        default_sheet = wb.active\n        wb.remove(default_sheet)\n\n        for sheet_name, rows in data.items():\n            ws = wb.create_sheet(title=sheet_name)\n            if not rows:\n                continue\n            # Default error categories can be customized\n        self.configurable_categories = categories if categories else {\n            "MissingValue": "A required value is missing.",\n            "TypeMismatch": "Data type does not match expected type.",\n            "OutOfRange": "Value is out of allowed range.",\n            "FormatError": "Value format is incorrect.",\n            "LogicError": "Logical inconsistency detected.",\n            "UnknownError": "Uncategorized error."\n        }\n\n    def categorize_errors(self, output_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> List[Dict[str, str]]:\n        """\n        Analyzes output_data and returns a list of categorized errors.\n        Each error is a dict: {"category": str, "description": str}\n        """\n        errors = []\n        for sheet_name, rows in output_data.items():\n            for row_idx, row in enumerate(rows):\n                for col, value in row.items():\n                    # import json
import sqlite3
from typing import Dict, Any, List, Optional, Tuple
import os

class RAGKnowledgeBase:
    """
    Handles RAG knowledge base operations:
    - log_test_data(client_id: str, input_data: dict, output_data: dict, errors: list)
    - retrieve_similar_cases(query: str, client_id: str) -> list
    - improve_input_generation(client_id: str) -> dict

    Uses local storage (SQLite/JSON) for per-client data isolation.
    """

    def __init__(self, storage):
        """
        storage: instance of Storage class (must provide get_storage_type(), get_storage_path())
        """
        self.storage = storage
        self.storage_type = self.storage.get_storage_type()
        self.storage_path = self.storage.get_storage_path()
        if self.storage_type == "sqlite":
            self._init_sqlite_db()
        elif self.storage_type == "json":
            self._init_json_db()
        else:
            raise ValueError("Unsupported storage type: {}".format(self.storage_type))

    # --- SQLite Implementation ---
    def _init_sqlite_db(self):
        self.conn = sqlite3.connect(self.storage_path)
        self.cursor = self.conn.cursor()
        self.cursor.execute("""
            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS rag_kb (
                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
                client_id TEXT NOT NULL,
                input_data TEXT,
                output_data TEXT,
                errors TEXT
            )
        """)
        self.conn.commit()

    # --- JSON Implementation ---
    def _init_json_db(self):
        if not os.path.exists(self.storage_path):
            with open(self.storage_path, "w") as f:
                json.dump({}, f)

    def log_test_data(self, client_id: str, input_data: Dict[str, Any], output_data: Dict[str, Any], errors: List[Dict[str, str]]):
        """
        Logs test data (input, output, errors) for a client into the knowledge base.
        """
        if self.storage_type == "sqlite":
            self.cursor.execute(
                "INSERT INTO rag_kb (client_id, input_data, output_data, errors) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)",
                (client_id, json.dumps(input_data), json.dumps(output_data), json.dumps(errors))
            )
            self.conn.commit()
        elif self.storage_type == "json":
            with open(self.storage_path, "r") as f:
                db = json.load(f)
            if client_id not in db:
                db[client_id] = []
            db[client_id].append({
                "input_data": input_data,
                "output_data": output_data,
                "errors": errors
            })
            with open(self.storage_path, "w") as f:
                json.dump(db, f, indent=2)

    def retrieve_similar_cases(self, query: str, client_id: str) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """
        Retrieves similar cases from the knowledge base for a given query and client.
        For simplicity, matches query against error descriptions.
        """
        results = []
        if self.storage_type == "sqlite":
            self.cursor.execute(
                "SELECT input_data, output_data, errors FROM rag_kb WHERE client_id=?",
                (client_id,)
            )
            rows = self.cursor.fetchall()
            for input_data, output_data, errors in rows:
                error_list = json.loads(errors)
                for error in error_list:
                    if query.lower() in error.get("description", "").lower():
                        results.append({
                            "input_data": json.loads(input_data),
                            "output_data": json.loads(output_data),
                            "error": error
                        })
        elif self.storage_type == "json":
            with open(self.storage_path, "r") as f:
                db = json.load(f)
            for case in db.get(client_id, []):
                for error in case.get("errors", []):
                    if query.lower() in error.get("description", "").lower():
                        results.append({
                            "input_data": case.get("input_data", {}),
                            "output_data": case.get("output_data", {}),
                            "error": error
                        })
        return results

    def improve_input_generation(self, client_id: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """
        Suggests improved input generation based on historical errors for the client.
        Returns a dict of suggestions (e.g., columns with frequent errors).
        """
        error_count = {}
        if self.storage_type == "sqlite":
            self.cursor.execute(
                "SELECT input_data, output_data, errors FROM rag_kb WHERE client_id=?",
                (client_id,)
            )
            rows = self.cursor.fetchall()
            for input_data, output_data, errors in rows:
                error_list = json.loads(errors)
                for error in error_list:
                    key = error.get("category", "UnknownError")
                    error_count[key] = error_count.get(key, 0) + 1
        elif self.storage_type == "json":
            with open(self.storage_path, "r") as f:
                db = json.load(f)
            for case in db.get(client_id, []):
                for error in case.get("errors", []):
                    key = error.get("category", "UnknownError")
                    error_count[key] = error_count.get(key, 0) + 1

        # Suggest improvements based on most frequent error categories
        if not error_count:
            return {"suggestion": "No historical errors found. No improvement needed."}
        sorted_errors = sorted(error_count.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
        suggestions = {
            "frequent_error_categories": sorted_errors,
            "suggestion": "Focus on resolving errors in the most frequent categories above."
        }
        return suggestions

    def __del__(self):
        if hasattr(self, "conn"):
            self.conn.close()

You may change this if using a different model\n\n    def generate_test_input(self, prompt: str) -> dict:\n        """\n        Sends a prompt to the GPT-4 API and returns the generated test input as a dictionary.\n        The response is expected to be a JSON string representing the test input data.\n        """\n        headers = {\n            "Authorization": f"Bearer {self.api_key}",\n            "Content-Type": "application/json"\n        }\n        payload = {\n            "model": self.model,\n            "messages": [\n                {"role": "system", "content": "You are a helpful assistant that generates test input data for automated test scenarios in JSON format."},\n                {"role": "user", "content": prompt}\n            ],\n            "temperature": 0.2,\n            "max_tokens": 2048\n        }\n\n        response = requests.post(self.api_url, headers=headers, data=json.dumps(payload))\n        if response.status_code != 200:\n            raise Exception(f"GPT-4 API request failed: {response.status_code} {response.text}")\n\n        result = response.json()\n        # Extract the content from the response\n        try:\n            content = result["choices"][0]["message"]["content"]\n            # Try to parse the content as JSON\n            test_input_data = json.loads(content)\n            if not isinstance(test_input_data, dict):\n                raise Exception("GPT-4 response is not a valid JSON object.")\n            return test_input_data\n        except (KeyError, IndexError, json.JSONDecodeError) as e:\n            raise Exception(f"Failed to parse GPT-4 response: {str(e)}")'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of gpt4_api.py has been completed according to the system design, providing the GPT4API class and the generate_test_input method. Provides create_input_excel(data: dict, path: str) and analyze_output_excel(path: str) -> dict methods."\n        }\n    }\n]\n```'}, {'role': 'user', 'content': 'Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/python_gpt4_test_automation_app/excel_manager.py has been successfully created, with content:\nimport openpyxl\nimport pandas as pd\nfrom typing import Dict, Any\n\nclass ExcelManager:\n    """\n    Handles creation and analysis of Excel files for input and output test scenarios.\n    Uses openpyxl for writing and pandas for reading/analyzing.\n    """\n\n    def create_input_excel(self, data: Dict[str, Any], path: str):\n        """\n        Creates an Excel file at the given path from the provided data dictionary.\n        The data dict should be structured as {sheet_name: [row_dict, ...], ...}\n        Each row_dict is a mapping of column_name to value.\n        """\n        wb = openpyxl.Workbook()\n        # Remove the default sheet\n        default_sheet = wb.active\n        wb.remove(default_sheet)\n\n        for sheet_name, rows in data.items():\n            ws = wb.create_sheet(title=sheet_name)\n            if not rows:\n                continue\n            # Get all column names from the first row\n            columns = list(rows[0].keys())\n            ws.append(columns)\n            for row in rows:\n                ws.append([row.get(col, "") for col in columns])\n        wb.save(path)\n\n    def analyze_output_excel(self, path: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        """\n        Analyzes the Excel file at the given path and returns its contents as a dictionary.\n        The returned dict is structured as {sheet_name: [row_dict, ...], ...}\n        Each row_dict is a mapping of column_name to value.\n        """\n        xls = pd.ExcelFile(path)\n        result = {}\n        for sheet_name in xls.sheet_names:\n            df = pd.read_excel(xls, sheet_name=sheet_name)\n            # The system will continuously improve input generation by leveraging historical data and error logs stored in the knowledge base.\\n004|\\n005|## File list\\n006|\\n007|- main.py\\n008|- ui.py\\n009|- +show_dashboard()\\n033|        +upload_file(client_id: str)\\n034|        +download_file(client_id: str)\\n035|        +show_error_report(client_id: str)\\n036|        +select_client(client_id: +retrieve_similar_cases(query: str, client_id: str) -> list\\n052|        +improve_input_generation(client_id: str) -> dict\\n053|    }\\n054|    class ClientManager {\\n055|        +authenticate(username: str, password: str) -> str\\n056|        +get_clients() -> list\\n057|        +add_client(client_info: dict)\\n058|    }\\n059|    class Storage {\\n060|        +save_data(client_id: str, data: dict)\\n061|        +load_data(client_id: str) -> dict\\n062|        +set_storage_type(type: str)\\n063|    }\\n064|    Main --> UI\\n065|    Main --> self.password_entry.get()\n        client_id = self.client_manager.authenticate(username, password)\n        if client_id:\n            self.current_client_id = client_id\n            self._build_dashboard()\n        else:\n            messagebox.showerror("Login Failed", "Invalid username or password.")\n\n    def _build_dashboard(self):\n        self._clear_root()\n        self.dashboard_frame = ttk.Frame(self.root, padding=10)\n        self.dashboard_frame.pack(fill="both", expand=True)\n\n        # Client selector\n        ttk.Label(self.dashboard_frame, text="Select
