You may change this if using a different model\n\n    def generate_test_input(self, prompt: str) -> dict:\n        """\n        Sends a prompt to the GPT-4 API and returns the generated test input as a dictionary.\n        The response is expected to be a JSON string representing the test input data.\n        """\n        headers = {\n            "Authorization": f"Bearer {self.api_key}",\n            "Content-Type": "application/json"\n        }\n        payload = {\n            "model": self.model,\n            "messages": [\n                {"role": "system", "content": "You are a helpful assistant that generates test input data for automated test scenarios in JSON format."},\n                {"role": "user", "content": prompt}\n            ],\n            "temperature": 0.2,\n            "max_tokens": 2048\n        }\n\n        response = requests.post(self.api_url, headers=headers, data=json.dumps(payload))\n        if response.status_code != 200:\n            raise Exception(f"GPT-4 API request failed: {response.status_code} {response.text}")\n\n        result = response.json()\n        # Extract the content from the response\n        try:\n            content = result["choices"][0]["message"]["content"]\n            # Try to parse the content as JSON\n            test_input_data = json.loads(content)\n            if not isinstance(test_input_data, dict):\n                raise Exception("GPT-4 response is not a valid JSON object.")\n            return test_input_data\n        except (KeyError, IndexError, json.JSONDecodeError) as e:\n            raise Exception(f"Failed to parse GPT-4 response: {str(e)}")'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of gpt4_api.py has been completed according to the system design, providing the GPT4API class and the generate_test_input method. Remove the default sheet\n        default_sheet = wb.active\n        wb.remove(default_sheet)\n\n        for sheet_name, rows in data.items():\n            ws = wb.create_sheet(title=sheet_name)\n            if not rows:\n                continue\n            # Get all column names from the first row\n            columns = list(rows[0].keys())\n            ws.append(columns)\n            for row in rows:\n                ws.append([row.get(col, "") for col in columns])\n        wb.save(path)\n\n    def analyze_output_excel(self, path: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        """\n        Analyzes the Excel file at the given path and returns its contents as a dictionary.\n        The returned dict is structured as {sheet_name: [row_dict, ...], ...}\n        Each row_dict is a mapping of column_name to value.\n        """\n        xls = pd.ExcelFile(path)\n        result = {}\n        for sheet_name in xls.sheet_names:\n            df = pd.read_excel(xls, sheet_name=sheet_name)\n            # Convert DataFrame to list of dicts\n            rows = df.to_dict(orient="records")\n            result[sheet_name] = rows\n        return result'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of excel_manager.py is complete. Default error categories can be customized\n        self.configurable_categories = categories if categories else {\n            "MissingValue": "A required value is missing.",\n            "TypeMismatch": "Data type does not match expected type.",\n            "OutOfRange": "Value is out of allowed range.",\n            "FormatError": "Value format is incorrect.",\n            "LogicError": "Logical inconsistency detected.",\n            "UnknownError": "Uncategorized error."\n        }\n\n    def categorize_errors(self, output_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> List[Dict[str, str]]:\n        """\n        Analyzes output_data and returns a list of categorized errors.\n        Each error is a dict: {"category": str, "description": str}\n        """\n        errors = []\n        for sheet_name, rows in output_data.items():\n            for row_idx, row in enumerate(rows):\n                for col, value in row.items():\n                    # Example error checks (can be extended/configured)\n                    if value is None or (isinstance(value, str) and value.strip() == ""):\n                        errors.append({\n                            "category": "MissingValue",\n                            "description": f"Sheet \'{sheet_name}\', Row {row_idx+2}, Column \'{col}\': {self.configurable_categories[\'MissingValue\']}"\n                        })\n                    elif isinstance(value, str) and value.lower() == "error":\n                        errors.append({\n                            "category": "LogicError",\n                            "description": f"Sheet \'{sheet_name}\', Row {row_idx+2}, Column \'{col}\': {self.configurable_categories[\'LogicError\']}"\n                        })\n                    elif isinstance(value, str) and not self._is_valid_format(value):\n                        errors.append({\n                            "category": "FormatError",\n                            "description": f"Sheet \'{sheet_name}\', Row {row_idx+2}, Column \'{col}\': {self.configurable_categories[\'FormatError\']}"\n                        })\n                    elif self._is_out_of_range(value):\n                        errors.append({\n                            "category": "OutOfRange",\n                            "description": f"Sheet \'{sheet_name}\', Row {row_idx+2}, Column \'{col}\': {self.configurable_categories[\'OutOfRange\']}"\n                        })\n                    # Add more error checks as needed\n\n        if not errors:\n            errors.append({\n                "category": "UnknownError",\n                "description": "No errors detected or unable to categorize errors."\n            })\n        return errors\n\n    def _is_valid_format(self, value: str) -> bool:\n        #               input_data TEXT,\n                output_data TEXT,\n                errors TEXT\n            )\n        """)\n        self.conn.commit()\n\n    # --- JSON Implementation ---\n    def _init_json_db(self):\n        if not os.path.exists(self.storage_path):\n            with open(self.storage_path, "w") as f:\n                json.dump({}, f)\n\n    def log_test_data(self, client_id: """\n        if self.storage_type == "sqlite":\n            self.cursor.execute(\n                "INSERT INTO rag_kb (client_id, input_data, output_data, errors) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)",\n "input_data": input_data,\n                "output_data": output_data,\n                "errors": errors\n            })\n            with open(self.storage_path, "w") as f:\n  )\n            rows = self.cursor.fetchall()\n            for input_data, output_data, errors in rows:\n                error_list = json.loads(errors)\n                for error in error_list:\n You may change this if using a different model\n\n    def generate_test_input(self, prompt: str) -> dict:\n        """\n        Sends a prompt to the GPT-4 API and returns the generated test input as a dictionary.\n        The response is expected to be a JSON string representing the test input data.\n        """\n        headers = {\n            "Authorization": f"Bearer {self.api_key}",\n            "Content-Type": "application/json"\n        }\n        payload = {\n            "model": self.model,\n            "messages": [\n                {"role": "system", "content": "You are a helpful assistant that generates test input data for automated test scenarios in JSON format."},\n                {"role": "user", "content": prompt}\n            ],\n            "temperature": 0.2,\n            "max_tokens": 2048\n        }\n\n        response = requests.post(self.api_url, headers=headers, data=json.dumps(payload))\n        if response.status_code != 200:\n            raise Exception(f"GPT-4 API request failed: {response.status_code} {response.text}")\n\n        result = response.json()\n        # Extract the content from the response\n        try:\n            content = result["choices"][0]["message"]["content"]\n            # Try to parse the content as JSON\n            test_input_data = json.loads(content)\n            if not isinstance(test_input_data, dict):\n                raise Exception("GPT-4 response is not a valid JSON object.")\n            return test_input_data\n        except (KeyError, IndexError, json.JSONDecodeError) as e:\n            raise Exception(f"Failed to parse GPT-4 response: {str(e)}")'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of gpt4_api.py has been completed according to the system design, providing the GPT4API class and the generate_test_input method. Remove the default sheet\n        default_sheet = wb.active\n        wb.remove(default_sheet)\n\n        for sheet_name, rows in data.items():\n            ws = wb.create_sheet(title=sheet_name)\n            if not rows:\n                continue\n            # Get all column names from the first row\n            columns = list(rows[0].keys())\n            ws.append(columns)\n            for row in rows:\n                ws.append([row.get(col, "") for col in columns])\n        wb.save(path)\n\n    def analyze_output_excel(self, path: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        """\n        Analyzes the Excel file at the given path and returns its contents as a dictionary.\n        The returned dict is structured as {sheet_name: [row_dict, ...], ...}\n        Each row_dict is a mapping of column_name to value.\n        """\n        xls = pd.ExcelFile(path)\n        result = {}\n        for sheet_name in xls.sheet_names:\n            df = pd.read_excel(xls, sheet_name=sheet_name)\n            # Convert DataFrame to list of dicts\n            rows = df.to_dict(orient="records")\n            result[sheet_name] = rows\n        return result'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of excel_manager.py is complete. Default error categories can be customized\n        self.configurable_categories = categories if categories else {\n            "MissingValue": "A required value is missing.",\n            "TypeMismatch": "Data type does not match expected type.",\n            "OutOfRange": "Value is out of allowed range.",\n            "FormatError": "Value format is incorrect.",\n            "LogicError": "Logical inconsistency detected.",\n            "UnknownError": "Uncategorized error."\n        }\n\n    def categorize_errors(self, output_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> List[Dict[str, str]]:\n        """\n        Analyzes output_data and returns a list of categorized errors.\n        Each error is a dict: {"category": str, "description": str}\n        """\n        errors = []\n        for sheet_name, rows in output_data.items():\n            for row_idx, row in enumerate(rows):\n                for col, value in row.items():\n                    # Example error checks (can be extended/configured)\n                    if value is None or (isinstance(value, str) and value.strip() == ""):\n                        errors.append({\n                            "category": "MissingValue",\n                            "description": f"Sheet \'{sheet_name}\', Row {row_idx+2}, Column \'{col}\': {self.configurable_categories[\'MissingValue\']}"\n                        })\n                    elif isinstance(value, str) and value.lower() == "error":\n                        errors.append({\n                            "category": "LogicError",\n                            "description": f"Sheet \'{sheet_name}\', Row {row_idx+2}, Column \'{col}\': {self.configurable_categories[\'LogicError\']}"\n                        })\n                    elif isinstance(value, str) and not self._is_valid_format(value):\n                        errors.append({\n                            "category": "FormatError",\n                            "description": f"Sheet \'{sheet_name}\', Row {row_idx+2}, Column \'{col}\': {self.configurable_categories[\'FormatError\']}"\n                        })\n                    elif self._is_out_of_range(value):\n                        errors.append({\n                            "category": "OutOfRange",\n                            "description": f"Sheet \'{sheet_name}\', Row {row_idx+2}, Column \'{col}\': {self.configurable_categories[\'OutOfRange\']}"\n                        })\n                    # Add more error checks as needed\n\n        if not errors:\n            errors.append({\n                "category": "UnknownError",\n                "description": "No errors detected or unable to categorize errors."\n            })\n        return errors\n\n    def _is_valid_format(self, value: str) -> bool:\n        # Example: check if value is a valid number or date string\n        # Extend this function for more format checks\n        try:\n            float(value)\n            return True\n        except ValueError:\n            pass\n        # Check for date format (YYYY-MM-DD)\n        import re\n        if re.match(r"^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$", value):\n            return True\n        return False\n\n    def _is_out_of_range(self, value: Any) -> bool:\n        # Example: check if numeric value is out of range\n        if isinstance(value, (int, float)):\n            if value < 0 or value > 1e6:  # Example range\n                return True\n        return False'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of error_categorizer.py is complete. import json
import sqlite3
from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Any
import os
import hashlib

class ClientManager:
    """
    Handles user authentication, client management, and data partitioning for multi-client support.
    Provides:
        - authenticate(username, password) -> str
        - get_clients() -> list
        - add_client(client_info: dict)
    """

    def __init__(self, storage):
        """
        storage: instance of Storage class (must provide get_storage_type(), get_storage_path())
        """
        self.storage = storage
        self.storage_type = self.storage.get_storage_type()
        self.storage_path = self.storage.get_storage_path()
        self._init_db()

    def _init_db(self):
        if self.storage_type == "sqlite":
            self.conn = sqlite3.connect(self.storage_path)
            self.cursor = self.conn.cursor()
            self.cursor.execute("""
                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS clients (
                    client_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
                    username TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
                    password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
                    info TEXT
                )
            """)
            self.conn.commit()
        elif self.storage_type == "json":
            if not os.path.exists(self.storage_path):
                with open(self.storage_path, "w") as f:
                    json.dump({"clients": {}}, f)
            else:
                with open(self.storage_path, "r") as f:
                    db = json.load(f)
                if "clients" not in db:
                    db["clients"] = {}
                    with open(self.storage_path, "w") as f:
                        json.dump(db, f, indent=2)
        else:
            raise ValueError("Unsupported storage type: {}".format(self.storage_type))

    def _hash_password(self, password: str) -> str:
        return hashlib.sha256(password.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()

    def authenticate(self, username: str, password: str) -> Optional[str]:
        """
        Authenticates a user and returns the client_id if successful, else None.
        """
        password_hash = self._hash_password(password)
        if self.storage_type == "sqlite":
            self.cursor.execute(
                "SELECT client_id, password_hash FROM clients WHERE username=?",
                (username,)
            )
            row = self.cursor.fetchone()
            if row and row[1] == password_hash:
                return row[0]
            return None
        elif self.storage_type == "json":
            with open(self.storage_path, "r") as f:
                db = json.load(f)
            for client_id, client in db.get("clients", {}).items():
                if client.get("username") == username and client.get("password_hash") == password_hash:
                    return client_id
            return None

    def get_clients(self) -> List[str]:
        """
        Returns a list of client IDs.
        """
        if self.storage_type == "sqlite":
            self.cursor.execute("SELECT client_id FROM clients")
            return [row[0] for row in self.cursor.fetchall()]
        elif self.storage_type == "json":
            with open(self.storage_path, "r") as f:
                db = json.load(f)
            return list(db.get("clients", {}).keys())

    def add_client(self, client_info: Dict[str, Any]):
        """
        Adds a new client. client_info must include 'client_id', 'username', 'password', and optionally other info.
        """
        client_id = client_info.get("client_id")
        username = client_info.get("username")
        password = client_info.get("password")
        info = {k: v for k, v in client_info.items() if k not in ["client_id", "username", "password"]}
        password_hash = self._hash_password(password)
        if not client_id or not username or not password:
            raise ValueError("client_id, username, and password are required to add a client.")

        if self.storage_type == "sqlite":
            try:
                self.cursor.execute(
                    "INSERT INTO clients (client_id, username, password_hash, info) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)",
                    (client_id, username, password_hash, json.dumps(info))
                )
                self.conn.commit()
            except sqlite3.IntegrityError as e:
                raise ValueError(f"Failed to add client: {str(e)}")
        elif self.storage_type == "json":
            with open(self.storage_path, "r") as f:
                db = json.load(f)
            if client_id in db.get("clients", {}):
                raise ValueError("Client ID already exists.")
            for cid, client in db.get("clients", {}).items():
                if client.get("username") == username:
                    raise ValueError("Username already exists.")
            db["clients"][client_id] = {
                "username": username,
                "password_hash": password_hash,
                "info": info
            }
            with open(self.storage_path, "w") as f:
                json.dump(db, f, indent=2)

    def __del__(self):
        if hasattr(self, "conn"):
            self.conn.close()

The app will use Tkinter for the UI (simple, cross-platform, and compatible with .exe builds). For GPT-4 API integration, we\'ll use the requests library. Excel file generation and analysis will be handled by openpyxl and pandas. Error categorization logic will be modular and configurable. Multi-client support will be managed via user authentication and client-specific data partitions. The system will continuously improve input generation by leveraging historical data and error logs stored in the knowledge base.\\n004|\\n005|## File list\\n006|\\n007|- main.py\\n008|- ui.py\\n009|- +show_dashboard()\\n033|        +upload_file(client_id: str)\\n034|        +download_file(client_id: str)\\n035|        +show_error_report(client_id: str)\\n036|        +select_client(client_id: +configurable_categories: dict\\n048|    }\\n049|    class RAGKnowledgeBase {\\n050|        +log_test_data(client_id: str, input_data: dict, output_data: dict, errors: list)\\n051|        +retrieve_similar_cases(query: str, client_id: str) -> list\\n052|        +improve_input_generation(client_id: str) -> dict\\n053|    }\\n054|    class ClientManager {\\n055|        +authenticate(username: str, password: str) -> str\\n056|        +get_clients() -> list\\n057|        +add_client(client_info: dict)\\n058|    }\\n059|    class Storage {\\n060|        +save_data(client_id: str, data: dict)\\n061|        +load_data(client_id: str) -> dict\\n062|        +set_storage_type(type: str)\\n063|    }\\n064|    Main --> UI\\n065|    Main --> ErrorCategorizer\\n072|    ErrorCategorizer --> RAGKnowledgeBase\\n073|    RAGKnowledgeBase --> Storage\\n074|    ClientManager --> Storage\\n075|```\\n076|\\n077|## Program call flow:\\n078|\\n079|```mermaid\\n080|sequenceDiagram\\n081|    participant M as Main\\n082|    participant U as UI\\n083|    participant CM as ClientManager\\n084|    participant S as Storage\\n085| __init__(self, root, client_manager, storage, config):\n        self.root = root\n        self.client_manager = client_manager\n        self.storage = storage\n        self.config = config\n\n        self.gpt4_api = GPT4API(self.config.get_gpt4_api_key())\n        self.excel_manager = ExcelManager()\n        self.error_categorizer = ErrorCategorizer()\n        self.rag_kb = _build_login_screen(self):\n        self._clear_root()\n        self.login_frame = ttk.Frame(self.root, padding=30)\n        self.login_frame.pack(expand=True)\n\n        ttk.Label(self.login_frame, text="Login", command=self._login)\n        self.login_btn.grid(row=3, column=0, columnspan=2, pady=15)\n\n    def _login(self):\n        username = self.username_entry.get()\n        password = self.password_entry.get()\n        client_id = self.client_manager.authenticate(username, password)\n        if client_id:\n            self.current_client_id = client_id\n            self._build_dashboard()\n        else:\n            messagebox.showerror("Login Failed", "Invalid username or password.")\n\n    def _build_dashboard(self):\n        self._clear_root()\n        self.dashboard_frame = ttk.Frame(self.root, padding=10)\n        self.dashboard_frame.pack(fill="both", expand=True)\n\n        # Client selector\n        ttk.Label(self.dashboard_frame, text="Select self.client_selector.set(self.current_client_id)\n        self.client_selector.grid(row=0, column=1, sticky="w")\n        self.client_selector.bind("<<ComboboxSelected>>", self._on_client_selected)\n\n        # File upload/download\n        ttk.Label(self.dashboard_frame, text="Input Excel File:").grid(row=1, column=0, sticky="w", pady=5)\n        self.upload_input_btn You may change this if using a different model\n\n    def generate_test_input(self, prompt: str) -> dict:\n        """\n        Sends a prompt to the GPT-4 API and returns the generated test input as a dictionary.\n        The response is expected to be a JSON string representing the test input data.\n        """\n        headers = {\n            "Authorization": f"Bearer {self.api_key}",\n            "Content-Type": "application/json"\n        }\n        payload = {\n            "model": self.model,\n            "messages": [\n                {"role": "system", "content": "You are a helpful assistant that generates test input data for automated test scenarios in JSON format."},\n                {"role": "user", "content": prompt}\n            ],\n            "temperature": 0.2,\n            "max_tokens": 2048\n        }\n\n        response = requests.post(self.api_url, headers=headers, data=json.dumps(payload))\n        if response.status_code != 200:\n            raise Exception(f"GPT-4 API request failed: {response.status_code} {response.text}")\n\n        result = response.json()\n        # Extract the content from the response\n        try:\n            content = result["choices"][0]["message"]["content"]\n            # Try to parse the content as JSON\n            test_input_data = json.loads(content)\n            if not isinstance(test_input_data, dict):\n                raise Exception("GPT-4 response is not a valid JSON object.")\n            return test_input_data\n        except (KeyError, IndexError, json.JSONDecodeError) as e:\n            raise Exception(f"Failed to parse GPT-4 response: {str(e)}")'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of gpt4_api.py has been completed according to the system design, providing the GPT4API class and the generate_test_input method. Remove the default sheet\n        default_sheet = wb.active\n        wb.remove(default_sheet)\n\n        for sheet_name, rows in data.items():\n            ws = wb.create_sheet(title=sheet_name)\n            if not rows:\n                continue\n            # Get all column names from the first row\n            columns = list(rows[0].keys())\n            ws.append(columns)\n            for row in rows:\n                ws.append([row.get(col, "") for col in columns])\n        wb.save(path)\n\n    def analyze_output_excel(self, path: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        """\n        Analyzes the Excel file at the given path and returns its contents as a dictionary.\n        The returned dict is structured as {sheet_name: [row_dict, ...], ...}\n        Each row_dict is a mapping of column_name to value.\n        """\n        xls = pd.ExcelFile(path)\n        result = {}\n        for sheet_name in xls.sheet_names:\n            df = pd.read_excel(xls, sheet_name=sheet_name)\n            # Convert DataFrame to list of dicts\n            rows = df.to_dict(orient="records")\n            result[sheet_name] = rows\n        return result'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of excel_manager.py is complete. Default error categories can be customized\n        self.configurable_categories = categories if categories else {\n            "MissingValue": "A required value is missing.",\n            "TypeMismatch": "Data type does not match expected type.",\n            "OutOfRange": "Value is out of allowed range.",\n            "FormatError": "Value format is incorrect.",\n            "LogicError": "Logical inconsistency detected.",\n            "UnknownError": "Uncategorized error."\n        }\n\n    def categorize_errors(self, output_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> List[Dict[str, str]]:\n        """\n        Analyzes output_data and returns a list of categorized errors.\n        Each error is a dict: {"category": str, "description": str}\n        """\n        errors = []\n        for sheet_name, rows in output_data.items():\n            for row_idx, row in enumerate(rows):\n                for col, value in row.items():\n                    # Example error checks (can be extended/configured)\n                    if value is None or (isinstance(value, str) and value.strip() == ""):\n                        errors.append({\n                            "category": "MissingValue",\n                            "description": f"Sheet \'{sheet_name}\', Row {row_idx+2}, Column \'{col}\': {self.configurable_categories[\'MissingValue\']}"\n                        })\n                    elif isinstance(value, str) and value.lower() == "error":\n                        errors.append({\n                            "category": "LogicError",\n                            "description": f"Sheet \'{sheet_name}\', Row {row_idx+2}, Column \'{col}\': {self.configurable_categories[\'LogicError\']}"\n                        })\n                    elif isinstance(value, str) and not self._is_valid_format(value):\n                        errors.append({\n                            "category": "FormatError",\n                            "description": f"Sheet \'{sheet_name}\', Row {row_idx+2}, Column \'{col}\': {self.configurable_categories[\'FormatError\']}"\n                        })\n                    elif self._is_out_of_range(value):\n                        errors.append({\n                            "category": "OutOfRange",\n                            "description": f"Sheet \'{sheet_name}\', Row {row_idx+2}, Column \'{col}\': {self.configurable_categories[\'OutOfRange\']}"\n                        })\n                    # Add more error checks as needed\n\n        if not errors:\n            errors.append({\n                "category": "UnknownError",\n                "description": "No errors detected or unable to categorize errors."\n            })\n        return errors\n\n    def _is_valid_format(self, value: str) -> bool:\n        # Example: check if value is a valid number or date string\n        # Extend this function for more format checks\n        try:\n            float(value)\n            return True\n        except ValueError:\n            pass\n        # _init_json_db(self):\n        if not os.path.exists(self.storage_path):\n            with open(self.storage_path, "w") as f:\n                json.dump({}, f)\n\n    def log_test_data(self, client_id: """\n        if self.storage_type == "sqlite":\n            self.cursor.execute(\n                "INSERT INTO rag_kb (client_id, input_data, output_data, errors) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)",\n  )\n            rows = self.cursor.fetchall()\n            for input_data, output_data, errors in rows:\n                error_list = json.loads(errors)\n                for error in error_list:\n You may change this if using a different model\n\n    def generate_test_input(self, prompt: str) -> dict:\n        """\n        Sends a prompt to the GPT-4 API and returns the generated test input as a dictionary.\n        The response is expected to be a JSON string representing the test input data.\n        """\n        headers = {\n            "Authorization": f"Bearer {self.api_key}",\n            "Content-Type": "application/json"\n        }\n        payload = {\n            "model": self.model,\n            "messages": [\n                {"role": "system", "content": "You are a helpful assistant that generates test input data for automated test scenarios in JSON format."},\n                {"role": "user", "content": prompt}\n            ],\n            "temperature": 0.2,\n            "max_tokens": 2048\n        }\n\n        response = requests.post(self.api_url, headers=headers, data=json.dumps(payload))\n        if response.status_code != 200:\n            raise Exception(f"GPT-4 API request failed: {response.status_code} {response.text}")\n\n        result = response.json()\n        # Extract the content from the response\n        try:\n            content = result["choices"][0]["message"]["content"]\n            # Try to parse the content as JSON\n            test_input_data = json.loads(content)\n            if not isinstance(test_input_data, dict):\n                raise Exception("GPT-4 response is not a valid JSON object.")\n            return test_input_data\n        except (KeyError, IndexError, json.JSONDecodeError) as e:\n            raise Exception(f"Failed to parse GPT-4 response: {str(e)}")'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of gpt4_api.py has been completed according to the system design, providing the GPT4API class and the generate_test_input method. Remove the default sheet\n        default_sheet = wb.active\n        wb.remove(default_sheet)\n\n        for sheet_name, rows in data.items():\n            ws = wb.create_sheet(title=sheet_name)\n            if not rows:\n                continue\n            # Get all column names from the first row\n            columns = list(rows[0].keys())\n            ws.append(columns)\n            for row in rows:\n                ws.append([row.get(col, "") for col in columns])\n        wb.save(path)\n\n    def analyze_output_excel(self, path: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        """\n        Analyzes the Excel file at the given path and returns its contents as a dictionary.\n        The returned dict is structured as {sheet_name: [row_dict, ...], ...}\n        Each row_dict is a mapping of column_name to value.\n        """\n        xls = pd.ExcelFile(path)\n        result = {}\n        for sheet_name in xls.sheet_names:\n            df = pd.read_excel(xls, sheet_name=sheet_name)\n            # import json
import sqlite3
import os
from typing import Dict, Any, Optional

class Storage:
    """
    Handles saving and loading data for each client using SQLite or JSON as the local storage backend.
    Provides:
        - save_data(client_id: str, data: dict)
        - load_data(client_id: str) -> dict
        - set_storage_type(type: str)
        - get_storage_type() -> str
        - get_storage_path() -> str
    """

    def __init__(self, storage_type: str = "sqlite", storage_path: str = "storage.db"):
        self._storage_type = storage_type.lower()
        self._storage_path = storage_path
        if self._storage_type == "sqlite":
            self._init_sqlite()
        elif self._storage_type == "json":
            self._init_json()
        else:
            raise ValueError(f"Unsupported storage type: {self._storage_type}")

    def _init_sqlite(self):
        self.conn = sqlite3.connect(self._storage_path)
        self.cursor = self.conn.cursor()
        self.cursor.execute("""
            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS client_data (
                client_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
                data TEXT
            )
        """)
        self.conn.commit()

    def _init_json(self):
        if not os.path.exists(self._storage_path):
            with open(self._storage_path, "w") as f:
                json.dump({}, f)

    def save_data(self, client_id: str, data: Dict[str, Any]):
        """
        Saves data for the given client_id.
        If data already exists, it updates/merges the new data.
        """
        if self._storage_type == "sqlite":
            # Load existing data if any
            self.cursor.execute("SELECT data FROM client_data WHERE client_id=?", (client_id,))
            row = self.cursor.fetchone()
            if row:
                existing_data = json.loads(row[0])
                existing_data.update(data)
                data_to_store = json.dumps(existing_data)
                self.cursor.execute("UPDATE client_data SET data=? WHERE client_id=?", (data_to_store, client_id))
            else:
                data_to_store = json.dumps(data)
                self.cursor.execute("INSERT INTO client_data (client_id, data) VALUES (?, ?)", (client_id, data_to_store))
            self.conn.commit()
        elif self._storage_type == "json":
            with open(self._storage_path, "r") as f:
                db = json.load(f)
            if client_id in db:
                db[client_id].update(data)
            else:
                db[client_id] = data
            with open(self._storage_path, "w") as f:
                json.dump(db, f, indent=2)

    def load_data(self, client_id: str) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """
        Loads and returns data for the given client_id.
        Returns None if no data is found.
        """
        if self._storage_type == "sqlite":
            self.cursor.execute("SELECT data FROM client_data WHERE client_id=?", (client_id,))
            row = self.cursor.fetchone()
            if row:
                try:
                    return json.loads(row[0])
                except Exception:
                    return None
            return None
        elif self._storage_type == "json":
            if not os.path.exists(self._storage_path):
                return None
            with open(self._storage_path, "r") as f:
                db = json.load(f)
            return db.get(client_id, None)

    def set_storage_type(self, type: str):
        """
        Changes the storage type (sqlite or json). Re-initializes the backend.
        """
        type = type.lower()
        if type not in ["sqlite", "json"]:
            raise ValueError("Storage type must be 'sqlite' or 'json'.")
        self._storage_type = type
        if type == "sqlite":
            self._init_sqlite()
        else:
            self._init_json()

    def get_storage_type(self) -> str:
        """
        Returns the current storage type.
        """
        return self._storage_type

    def get_storage_path(self) -> str:
        """
        Returns the current storage path.
        """
        return self._storage_path

    def __del__(self):
        if hasattr(self, "conn"):
            self.conn.close()

You may change this if using a different model\n\n    def generate_test_input(self, prompt: str) -> dict:\n        """\n        Sends a prompt to the GPT-4 API and returns the generated test input as a dictionary.\n        The response is expected to be a JSON string representing the test input data.\n        """\n        headers = {\n            "Authorization": f"Bearer {self.api_key}",\n            "Content-Type": "application/json"\n        }\n        payload = {\n            "model": self.model,\n            "messages": [\n                {"role": "system", "content": "You are a helpful assistant that generates test input data for automated test scenarios in JSON format."},\n                {"role": "user", "content": prompt}\n            ],\n            "temperature": 0.2,\n            "max_tokens": 2048\n        }\n\n        response = requests.post(self.api_url, headers=headers, data=json.dumps(payload))\n        if response.status_code != 200:\n            raise Exception(f"GPT-4 API request failed: {response.status_code} {response.text}")\n\n        result = response.json()\n        # Extract the content from the response\n        try:\n            content = result["choices"][0]["message"]["content"]\n            # Try to parse the content as JSON\n            test_input_data = json.loads(content)\n            if not isinstance(test_input_data, dict):\n                raise Exception("GPT-4 response is not a valid JSON object.")\n            return test_input_data\n        except (KeyError, IndexError, json.JSONDecodeError) as e:\n            raise Exception(f"Failed to parse GPT-4 response: {str(e)}")'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of gpt4_api.py has been completed according to the system design, providing the GPT4API class and the generate_test_input method. Provides create_input_excel(data: dict, path: str) and analyze_output_excel(path: str) -> dict methods."\n        }\n    }\n]\n```'}, {'role': 'user', 'content': 'Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/python_gpt4_test_automation_app/excel_manager.py has been successfully created, with content:\nimport openpyxl\nimport pandas as pd\nfrom typing import Dict, Any\n\nclass ExcelManager:\n    """\n    Handles creation and analysis of Excel files for input and output test scenarios.\n    Uses openpyxl for writing and pandas for reading/analyzing.\n    """\n\n    def create_input_excel(self, data: Dict[str, Any], path: str):\n        """\n        Creates an Excel file at the given path from the provided data dictionary.\n        The data dict should be structured as {sheet_name: [row_dict, ...], ...}\n        Each row_dict is a mapping of column_name to value.\n        """\n        wb = openpyxl.Workbook()\n        # Remove the default sheet\n        default_sheet = wb.active\n        wb.remove(default_sheet)\n\n        for sheet_name, rows in data.items():\n            ws = wb.create_sheet(title=sheet_name)\n            if not rows:\n                continue\n            # Get all column names from the first row\n            columns = list(rows[0].keys())\n            ws.append(columns)\n            for row in rows:\n                ws.append([row.get(col, "") for col in columns])\n        wb.save(path)\n\n    def analyze_output_excel(self, path: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        """\n        Analyzes the Excel file at the given path and returns its contents as a dictionary.\n        The returned dict is structured as {sheet_name: [row_dict, ...], ...}\n        Each row_dict is a mapping of column_name to value.\n        """\n        xls = pd.ExcelFile(path)\n        result = {}\n        for sheet_name in xls.sheet_names:\n            df = pd.read_excel(xls, sheet_name=sheet_name)\n            # You may change this if using a different model\n\n    def generate_test_input(self, prompt: str) -> dict:\n        """\n        Sends a prompt to the GPT-4 API and returns the generated test input as a dictionary.\n        The response is expected to be a JSON string representing the test input data.\n        """\n        headers = {\n            "Authorization": f"Bearer {self.api_key}",\n            "Content-Type": "application/json"\n        }\n        payload = {\n            "model": self.model,\n            "messages": [\n                {"role": "system", "content": "You are a helpful assistant that generates test input data for automated test scenarios in JSON format."},\n                {"role": "user", "content": prompt}\n            ],\n            "temperature": 0.2,\n            "max_tokens": 2048\n        }\n\n        response = requests.post(self.api_url, headers=headers, data=json.dumps(payload))\n        if response.status_code != 200:\n            raise Exception(f"GPT-4 API request failed: {response.status_code} {response.text}")\n\n        result = response.json()\n        # Extract the content from the response\n        try:\n            content = result["choices"][0]["message"]["content"]\n            # Try to parse the content as JSON\n            test_input_data = json.loads(content)\n            if not isinstance(test_input_data, dict):\n                raise Exception("GPT-4 response is not a valid JSON object.")\n            return test_input_data\n        except (KeyError, IndexError, json.JSONDecodeError) as e:\n            raise Exception(f"Failed to parse GPT-4 response: {str(e)}")'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of gpt4_api.py has been completed according to the system design, providing the GPT4API class and the generate_test_input method. Remove the default sheet\n        default_sheet = wb.active\n        wb.remove(default_sheet)\n\n        for sheet_name, rows in data.items():\n            ws = wb.create_sheet(title=sheet_name)\n            if not rows:\n                continue\n            # Get all column names from the first row\n            columns = list(rows[0].keys())\n            ws.append(columns)\n            for row in rows:\n                ws.append([row.get(col, "") for col in columns])\n        wb.save(path)\n\n    def analyze_output_excel(self, path: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        """\n        Analyzes the Excel file at the given path and returns its contents as a dictionary.\n        The returned dict is structured as {sheet_name: [row_dict, ...], ...}\n        Each row_dict is a mapping of column_name to value.\n        """\n        xls = pd.ExcelFile(path)\n        result = {}\n        for sheet_name in xls.sheet_names:\n            df = pd.read_excel(xls, sheet_name=sheet_name)\n            # Convert DataFrame to list of dicts\n            rows = df.to_dict(orient="records")\n            result[sheet_name] = rows\n        return result'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of excel_manager.py is complete. Default error categories can be customized\n        self.configurable_categories = categories if categories else {\n            "MissingValue": "A required value is missing.",\n            "TypeMismatch": "Data type does not match expected type.",\n            "OutOfRange": "Value is out of allowed range.",\n            "FormatError": "Value format is incorrect.",\n            "LogicError": "Logical inconsistency detected.",\n            "UnknownError": "Uncategorized error."\n        }\n\n    def categorize_errors(self, output_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> List[Dict[str, str]]:\n        """\n        Analyzes output_data and returns a list of categorized errors.\n        Each error is a dict: {"category": str, "description": str}\n        """\n        errors = []\n        for sheet_name, rows in output_data.items():\n            for row_idx, row in enumerate(rows):\n                for col, value in row.items():\n                    # Example error checks (can be extended/configured)\n                    if value is None or (isinstance(value, str) and value.strip() == ""):\n                        errors.append({\n                            "category": "MissingValue",\n                            "description": f"Sheet \'{sheet_name}\', Row {row_idx+2}, Column \'{col}\': {self.configurable_categories[\'MissingValue\']}"\n                        })\n                    elif isinstance(value, str) and value.lower() == "error":\n                        errors.append({\n                            "category": "LogicError",\n                            "description": f"Sheet \'{sheet_name}\', Row {row_idx+2}, Column \'{col}\': {self.configurable_categories[\'LogicError\']}"\n                        })\n                    elif isinstance(value, str) and not self._is_valid_format(value):\n                        errors.append({\n                            "category": "FormatError",\n                            "description": f"Sheet \'{sheet_name}\', Row {row_idx+2}, Column \'{col}\': {self.configurable_categories[\'FormatError\']}"\n                        })\n                    elif self._is_out_of_range(value):\n                        errors.append({\n                            "category": "OutOfRange",\n                            "description": f"Sheet \'{sheet_name}\', Row {row_idx+2}, Column \'{col}\': {self.configurable_categories[\'OutOfRange\']}"\n                        })\n                    # Add more error checks as needed\n\n        if not errors:\n            errors.append({\n                "category": "UnknownError",\n                "description": "No errors detected or unable to categorize errors."\n            })\n        return errors\n\n    def _is_valid_format(self, value: str) -> bool:\n        # Example: check if value is a valid number or date string\n        # Extend this function for more format checks\n        try:\n            float(value)\n            return True\n        except ValueError:\n            pass\n        # Check for date format (YYYY-MM-DD)\n        import re\n        if re.match(r"^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$", value):\n            return True\n        return False\n\n    def _is_out_of_range(self, value: Any) -> bool:\n        # Example: check if numeric value is out of range\n        if isinstance(value, (int, float)):\n            if value < 0 or value > 1e6:  # Example range\n                return True\n        return False'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of error_categorizer.py is complete. executed: The file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/python_gpt4_test_automation_app/rag_knowledge_base.py has been successfully created, with content:\nimport json\nimport sqlite3\nfrom typing import Dict, Any, List, Optional, Tuple\nimport os\n\nclass RAGKnowledgeBase:\n    """\n    Handles RAG knowledge base operations:\n    - log_test_data(client_id: str, input_data: dict, output_data: dict, errors: list)\n    - retrieve_similar_cases(query: str, client_id: str) -> list\n    - improve_input_generation(client_id: str) -> dict\n\n    Uses local storage (SQLite/JSON) for per-client data isolation.\n    """\n\n    def __init__(self, storage):\n        """\n        storage: instance of Storage class (must provide get_storage_type(), get_storage_path())\n        """\n        self.storage = storage\n        self.storage_type = self.storage.get_storage_type()\n        self.storage_path = self.storage.get_storage_path()\n        if self.storage_type == "sqlite":\n            self._init_sqlite_db()\n        elif self.storage_type == "json":\n            self._init_json_db()\n        else:\n            raise ValueError("Unsupported storage type: {}".format(self.storage_type))\n\n    # --- SQLite Implementation ---\n    def _init_sqlite_db(self):\n        self.conn = sqlite3.connect(self.storage_path)\n        self.cursor = self.conn.cursor()\n        self.cursor.execute("""\n            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS rag_kb (\n                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n                client_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n                input_data TEXT,\n                output_data TEXT,\n                errors TEXT\n            )\n        """)\n        self.conn.commit()\n\n    # --- JSON Implementation ---\n    def _init_json_db(self):\n        if not os.path.exists(self.storage_path):\n            with open(self.storage_path, "w") as f:\n                json.dump({}, f)\n\n    def log_test_data(self, client_id: str, input_data: Dict[str, Any], output_data: Dict[str, Any], errors: List[Dict[str, str]]):\n        """\n        Logs test data (input, output, errors) for a client into the knowledge base.\n        """\n        if self.storage_type == "sqlite":\n            self.cursor.execute(\n                "INSERT INTO rag_kb (client_id, input_data, output_data, errors) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)",\n                (client_id, json.dumps(input_data), json.dumps(output_data), json.dumps(errors))\n            )\n            self.conn.commit()\n        elif self.storage_type == "json":\n            with open(self.storage_path, "r") as f:\n                db = json.load(f)\n            if client_id not in db:\n                db[client_id] = []\n            db[client_id].append({\n                "input_data": input_data,\n                "output_data": output_data,\n                "errors": errors\n            })\n            with open(self.storage_path, "w") as f:\n                json.dump(db, f, indent=2)\n\n    def retrieve_similar_cases(self, query: str, client_id: str) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:\n        """\n        Retrieves similar cases from the knowledge base for a Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/python_gpt4_test_automation_app/config.py has been successfully created, with content:
import os
import json

class Config:
    """
    Handles configuration management for the application, including storage type, storage path, and GPT-4 API key.
    Provides:
        - get_storage_type()
        - get_storage_path()
        - get_gpt4_api_key()
    """

    DEFAULT_CONFIG_PATH = "config.json"
    DEFAULT_STORAGE_TYPE = "sqlite"
    DEFAULT_STORAGE_PATH = "storage.db"
    DEFAULT_GPT4_API_KEY = ""

    def __init__(self, config_path: str = None):
        self.config_path = config_path or self.DEFAULT_CONFIG_PATH
        self._config = self._load_config()

    def _load_config(self):
        # If config file does not exist, create with defaults
        if not os.path.exists(self.config_path):
            config = {
                "storage_type": self.DEFAULT_STORAGE_TYPE,
                "storage_path": self.DEFAULT_STORAGE_PATH,
                "gpt4_api_key": self.DEFAULT_GPT4_API_KEY
            }
            self._save_config(config)
            return config
        else:
            with open(self.config_path, "r") as f:
                try:
                    config = json.load(f)
                except Exception:
                    config = {
                        "storage_type": self.DEFAULT_STORAGE_TYPE,
                        "storage_path": self.DEFAULT_STORAGE_PATH,
                        "gpt4_api_key": self.DEFAULT_GPT4_API_KEY
                    }
            # Ensure all keys exist
            for key, default in [
                ("storage_type", self.DEFAULT_STORAGE_TYPE),
                ("storage_path", self.DEFAULT_STORAGE_PATH),
                ("gpt4_api_key", self.DEFAULT_GPT4_API_KEY)
            ]:
                if key not in config:
                    config[key] = default
            return config

    def _save_config(self, config):
        with open(self.config_path, "w") as f:
            json.dump(config, f, indent=2)

    def get_storage_type(self) -> str:
        """
        Returns the configured storage type ('sqlite' or 'json').
        """
        return self._config.get("storage_type", self.DEFAULT_STORAGE_TYPE).lower()

    def get_storage_path(self) -> str:
        """
        Returns the configured storage path.
        """
        return self._config.get("storage_path", self.DEFAULT_STORAGE_PATH)

    def get_gpt4_api_key(self) -> str:
        """
        Returns the configured GPT-4 API key.
        """
        return self._config.get("gpt4_api_key", self.DEFAULT_GPT4_API_KEY)

    def set_storage_type(self, storage_type: str):
        """
        Sets and saves the storage type.
        """
        self._config["storage_type"] = storage_type.lower()
        self._save_config(self._config)

    def set_storage_path(self, storage_path: str):
        """
        Sets and saves the storage path.
        """
        self._config["storage_path"] = storage_path
        self._save_config(self._config)

    def set_gpt4_api_key(self, api_key: str):
        """
        Sets and saves the GPT-4 API key.
        """
        self._config["gpt4_api_key"] = api_key
        self._save_config(self._config)

And end loop when all requirements are met.\nYou are an autonomous programmer\n\nThe special interface consists of a file editor that shows you 100 lines of a file at a time.\n\nYou can use terminal commands (e.g., cat, ls, cd) by calling Terminal.run_command.\n\nYou should carefully observe the behavior and results of the previous action, and avoid triggering repeated errors.\n\nIn addition to the terminal, I also provide additional tools. \n\nIf provided an issue link, you first action must be navigate to the issue page using Browser tool to understand the issue.\n\nYour must check if the repository exists at the current path. If it exists, navigate to the repository path. If the repository doesn\'t exist, please download it and then navigate to it.\nAll subsequent actions must be performed within this repository path. Do not leave this directory to execute any actions at any time.\n\nNote:\n\n1. If you open a file and need to get to an area around a specific line that is not in the first 100 lines, say line 583, don\'t just use the scroll_down command multiple times. Instead, use the Editor.goto_line command. It\'s much quicker. \n2. Always make sure to look at the currently open file and the current working directory (which appears right after the currently open file). The currently open file might be in a different directory than the working directory! Note that some commands, such as \'create\', open files, so they might change the current open file.\n3. When using Editor.edit_file_by_replace, if there is no exact match, take the difference in indentation into consideration.\n4. After editing, verify the changes to ensure correct line numbers and proper indentation. Adhere to PEP8 standards for Python code.\n5. NOTE ABOUT THE EDIT COMMAND: Indentation really matters! When editing a file, make sure to insert appropriate indentation before each line! Ensuring the code adheres to PEP8 standards. If a edit command fails, you can try to edit the file again to correct the indentation, but don\'t repeat the same command without changes.\n6. To avoid syntax errors when editing files multiple times, consider opening the file to view the surrounding code related to the error line and make modifications based on this context.\n7. Ensure to observe the currently open file and the current working directory, which is displayed right after the open file. The open file might be in a different directory than the working directory. Remember, commands like \'create\' open files and might alter the current open file.\n8. Effectively using Use search commands (`search_dir`, `search_file`, `find_file`) and navigation commands (`open_file`, `goto_line`) to locate and modify files efficiently. The Editor tool can fully satisfy the requirements. Follow these steps and considerations for optimal results:\n\n9. When the edit fails, try to enlarge the range of code.\n10. You must use the Editor.open_file command to open a file before using the Editor tool\'s edit command to modify it. When you open a file, any currently open file will be automatically closed.\n11. Remember, when you use Editor.insert_content_at_line or Editor.edit_file_by_replace, the line numbers will change after the operation. You may change this if using a different model\n\n    def generate_test_input(self, prompt: str) -> dict:\n        """\n        Sends a prompt to the GPT-4 API and returns the generated test input as a dictionary.\n        The response is expected to be a JSON string representing the test input data.\n        """\n        headers = {\n            "Authorization": f"Bearer {self.api_key}",\n            "Content-Type": "application/json"\n        }\n        payload = {\n            "model": self.model,\n            "messages": [\n                {"role": "system", "content": "You are a helpful assistant that generates test input data for automated test scenarios in JSON format."},\n                {"role": "user", "content": prompt}\n            ],\n            "temperature": 0.2,\n            "max_tokens": 2048\n        }\n\n        response = requests.post(self.api_url, headers=headers, data=json.dumps(payload))\n        if response.status_code != 200:\n            raise Exception(f"GPT-4 API request failed: {response.status_code} {response.text}")\n\n        result = response.json()\n        # Extract the content from the response\n        try:\n            content = result["choices"][0]["message"]["content"]\n            # Try to parse the content as JSON\n            test_input_data = json.loads(content)\n            if not isinstance(test_input_data, dict):\n                raise Exception("GPT-4 response is not a valid JSON object.")\n            return test_input_data\n        except (KeyError, IndexError, json.JSONDecodeError) as e:\n            raise Exception(f"Failed to parse GPT-4 response: {str(e)}")'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of gpt4_api.py has been completed according to the system design, providing the GPT4API class and the generate_test_input method. Remove the default sheet\n        default_sheet = wb.active\n        wb.remove(default_sheet)\n\n        for sheet_name, rows in data.items():\n            ws = wb.create_sheet(title=sheet_name)\n            if not rows:\n                continue\n            # Get all column names from the first row\n            columns = list(rows[0].keys())\n            ws.append(columns)\n            for row in rows:\n                ws.append([row.get(col, "") for col in columns])\n        wb.save(path)\n\n    def analyze_output_excel(self, path: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        """\n        Analyzes the Excel file at the given path and returns its contents as a dictionary.\n        The returned dict is structured as {sheet_name: [row_dict, ...], ...}\n        Each row_dict is a mapping of column_name to value.\n        """\n        xls = pd.ExcelFile(path)\n        result = {}\n        for sheet_name in xls.sheet_names:\n            df = pd.read_excel(xls, sheet_name=sheet_name)\n            # Convert DataFrame to list of dicts\n            rows = df.to_dict(orient="records")\n            result[sheet_name] = rows\n        return result'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of excel_manager.py is complete. Default error categories can be customized\n        self.configurable_categories = categories if categories else {\n            "MissingValue": "A required value is missing.",\n            "TypeMismatch": "Data type does not match expected type.",\n            "OutOfRange": "Value is out of allowed range.",\n            "FormatError": "Value format is incorrect.",\n            "LogicError": "Logical inconsistency detected.",\n            "UnknownError": "Uncategorized error."\n        }\n\n    def categorize_errors(self, output_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> List[Dict[str, str]]:\n        """\n        Analyzes output_data and returns a list of categorized errors.\n        Each error is a dict: {"category": str, "description": str}\n        """\n        errors = []\n        for sheet_name, rows in output_data.items():\n            for row_idx, row in enumerate(rows):\n                for col, value in row.items():\n                    # Example error checks (can be extended/configured)\n                    if value is None or (isinstance(value, str) and value.strip() == ""):\n                        errors.append({\n                            "category": "MissingValue",\n                            "description": f"Sheet \'{sheet_name}\', Row {row_idx+2}, Column \'{col}\': {self.configurable_categories[\'MissingValue\']}"\n                        })\n                    elif isinstance(value, str) and value.lower() == "error":\n                        errors.append({\n                            "category": "LogicError",\n                            "description": f"Sheet \'{sheet_name}\', Row {row_idx+2}, Column \'{col}\': {self.configurable_categories[\'LogicError\']}"\n                        })\n                    elif isinstance(value, str) and not self._is_valid_format(value):\n                        errors.append({\n                            "category": "FormatError",\n                            "description": f"Sheet \'{sheet_name}\', Row {row_idx+2}, Column \'{col}\': {self.configurable_categories[\'FormatError\']}"\n                        })\n                    elif self._is_out_of_range(value):\n                        errors.append({\n                            "category": "OutOfRange",\n                            "description": f"Sheet \'{sheet_name}\', Row {row_idx+2}, Column \'{col}\': {self.configurable_categories[\'OutOfRange\']}"\n                        })\n                    # Add more error checks as needed\n\n        if not errors:\n            errors.append({\n                "category": "UnknownError",\n                "description": "No errors detected or unable to categorize errors."\n            })\n        return errors\n\n    def _is_valid_format(self, value: str) -> bool:\n        # Example: check if value is a valid number or date string\n        # Extend this function for more format checks\n        try:\n            float(value)\n            return True\n        except ValueError:\n            pass\n        # Check for date format (YYYY-MM-DD)\n        import re\n        if re.match(r"^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$", value):\n            return True\n        return False\n\n    def _is_out_of_range(self, value: Any) -> bool:\n        # Example: check if numeric value is out of range\n        if isinstance(value, (int, float)):\n            if value < 0 or value > 1e6:  # Example range\n                return True\n        return False'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of error_categorizer.py is complete. You may change this if using a different model\n\n    def generate_test_input(self, prompt: str) -> dict:\n        """\n        Sends a prompt to the GPT-4 API and returns the generated test input as a dictionary.\n        The response is expected to be a JSON string representing the test input data.\n        """\n        headers = {\n            "Authorization": f"Bearer {self.api_key}",\n            "Content-Type": "application/json"\n        }\n        payload = {\n            "model": self.model,\n            "messages": [\n                {"role": "system", "content": "You are a helpful assistant that generates test input data for automated test scenarios in JSON format."},\n                {"role": "user", "content": prompt}\n            ],\n            "temperature": 0.2,\n            "max_tokens": 2048\n        }\n\n        response = requests.post(self.api_url, headers=headers, data=json.dumps(payload))\n        if response.status_code != 200:\n            raise Exception(f"GPT-4 API request failed: {response.status_code} {response.text}")\n\n        result = response.json()\n        # Extract the content from the response\n        try:\n            content = result["choices"][0]["message"]["content"]\n            # Try to parse the content as JSON\n            test_input_data = json.loads(content)\n            if not isinstance(test_input_data, dict):\n                raise Exception("GPT-4 response is not a valid JSON object.")\n            return test_input_data\n        except (KeyError, IndexError, json.JSONDecodeError) as e:\n            raise Exception(f"Failed to parse GPT-4 response: {str(e)}")'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of gpt4_api.py has been completed according to the system design, providing the GPT4API class and the generate_test_input method. Remove the default sheet\n        default_sheet = wb.active\n        wb.remove(default_sheet)\n\n        for sheet_name, rows in data.items():\n            ws = wb.create_sheet(title=sheet_name)\n            if not rows:\n                continue\n            # Get all column names from the first row\n            columns = list(rows[0].keys())\n            ws.append(columns)\n            for row in rows:\n                ws.append([row.get(col, "") for col in columns])\n        wb.save(path)\n\n    def analyze_output_excel(self, path: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        """\n        Analyzes the Excel file at the given path and returns its contents as a dictionary.\n        The returned dict is structured as {sheet_name: [row_dict, ...], ...}\n        Each row_dict is a mapping of column_name to value.\n        """\n        xls = pd.ExcelFile(path)\n        result = {}\n        for sheet_name in xls.sheet_names:\n            df = pd.read_excel(xls, sheet_name=sheet_name)\n            # Convert DataFrame to list of dicts\n            rows = df.to_dict(orient="records")\n            result[sheet_name] = rows\n        return result'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of excel_manager.py is complete. Default error categories can be customized\n        self.configurable_categories = categories if categories else {\n            "MissingValue": "A required value is missing.",\n            "TypeMismatch": "Data type does not match expected type.",\n            "OutOfRange": "Value is out of allowed range.",\n            "FormatError": "Value format is incorrect.",\n            "LogicError": "Logical inconsistency detected.",\n            "UnknownError": "Uncategorized error."\n        }\n\n    def categorize_errors(self, output_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> List[Dict[str, str]]:\n        """\n        Analyzes output_data and returns a list of categorized errors.\n        Each error is a dict: {"category": str, "description": str}\n        """\n        errors = []\n        for sheet_name, rows in output_data.items():\n            for row_idx, row in enumerate(rows):\n                for col, value in row.items():\n                    # Example error checks (can be extended/configured)\n                    if value is None or (isinstance(value, str) and value.strip() == ""):\n                        errors.append({\n                            "category": "MissingValue",\n                            "description": f"Sheet \'{sheet_name}\', Row {row_idx+2}, Column \'{col}\': {self.configurable_categories[\'MissingValue\']}"\n                        })\n                    elif isinstance(value, str) and value.lower() == "error":\n                        errors.append({\n                            "category": "LogicError",\n                            "description": f"Sheet \'{sheet_name}\', Row {row_idx+2}, Column \'{col}\': {self.configurable_categories[\'LogicError\']}"\n                        })\n                    elif isinstance(value, str) and not self._is_valid_format(value):\n                        errors.append({\n                            "category": "FormatError",\n                            "description": f"Sheet \'{sheet_name}\', Row {row_idx+2}, Column \'{col}\': {self.configurable_categories[\'FormatError\']}"\n                        })\n                    elif self._is_out_of_range(value):\n                        errors.append({\n                            "category": "OutOfRange",\n                            "description": f"Sheet \'{sheet_name}\', Row {row_idx+2}, Column \'{col}\': {self.configurable_categories[\'OutOfRange\']}"\n                        })\n                    # Add more error checks as needed\n\n        if not errors:\n            errors.append({\n                "category": "UnknownError",\n                "description": "No errors detected or unable to categorize errors."\n            })\n        return errors\n\n    def _is_valid_format(self, value: str) -> bool:\n        # Example: check if value is a valid number or date string\n        # Extend this function for more format checks\n        try:\n            float(value)\n            return True\n        except ValueError:\n            pass\n        # Check for date format (YYYY-MM-DD)\n        import re\n        if re.match(r"^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$", value):\n            return True\n        return False\n\n    def _is_out_of_range(self, value: Any) -> bool:\n        # Example: check if numeric value is out of range\n        if isinstance(value, (int, float)):\n            if value < 0 or value > 1e6:  # Example range\n                return True\n        return False'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of error_categorizer.py is complete. typing import Dict, Any, List, Optional, Tuple\nimport os\n\nclass RAGKnowledgeBase:\n    """\n    Handles RAG knowledge base operations:\n    - log_test_data(client_id: str, input_data: dict, output_data: dict, errors: list)\n    - retrieve_similar_cases(query: str, client_id: str) -> list\n    - improve_input_generation(client_id: str) -> dict\n\n    Uses local storage (SQLite/JSON) for per-client data isolation.\n    """\n\n    def __init__(self, storage):\n        """\n        storage: instance of Storage class (must provide get_storage_type(), get_storage_path())\n        """\n        self.storage = storage\n        self.storage_type = self.storage.get_storage_type()\n        self.storage_path = self.storage.get_storage_path()\n        if self.storage_type == "sqlite":\n            self._init_sqlite_db()\n        elif self.storage_type == "json":\n            self._init_json_db()\n        else:\n            raise ValueError("Unsupported storage type: {}".format(self.storage_type))\n\n    # --- SQLite Implementation ---\n    def _init_sqlite_db(self):\n        self.conn = sqlite3.connect(self.storage_path)\n        self.cursor = self.conn.cursor()\n        self.cursor.execute("""\n            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS rag_kb (\n                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n                client_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n                input_data TEXT,\n                output_data TEXT,\n                errors TEXT\n            )\n        """)\n        self.conn.commit()\n\n    # --- JSON Implementation ---\n    def _init_json_db(self):\n        if not os.path.exists(self.storage_path):\n            with open(self.storage_path, "w") as f:\n                json.dump({}, f)\n\n    def log_test_data(self, client_id: str, input_data: Dict[str, Any], output_data: Dict[str, Any], errors: List[Dict[str, str]]):\n        """\n        Logs test data (input, output, errors) for a client into the knowledge base.\n        """\n        if self.storage_type == "sqlite":\n            self.cursor.execute(\n                "INSERT INTO rag_kb (client_id, input_data, output_data, errors) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)",\n                (client_id, json.dumps(input_data), json.dumps(output_data), json.dumps(errors))\n            )\n            self.conn.commit()\n        elif self.storage_type == "json":\n            with open(self.storage_path, "r") as f:\n                db = json.load(f)\n            if client_id not in db:\n                db[client_id] = []\n            db[client_id].append({\n                "input_data": input_data,\n                "output_data": output_data,\n                "errors": errors\n            })\n            with open(self.storage_path, "w") as f:\n                json.dump(db, f, indent=2)\n\n    def retrieve_similar_cases(self, query: str, client_id: str) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:\n        """\n        Retrieves similar cases from the knowledge base for a given query and client.\n        For simplicity, matches query against error descriptions.\n        """\n        results = []\n        if self.storage_type == "sqlite":\n            self.cursor.execute(\n                "SELECT input_data, output_data, errors FROM rag_kb WHERE client_id=?",\n                (client_id,)\n            )\n            rows = self.cursor.fetchall()\n            for input_data, output_data, errors in rows:\n                error_list = json.loads(errors)\n                for error in error_list:\n                    if query.lower() in error.get("description", "").lower():\n                        results.append({\n                            "input_data": json.loads(input_data),\n                            "output_data": json.loads(output_data),\n                            "error": error\n                        })\n        elif self.storage_type == "json":\n            with open(self.storage_path, "r") as f:\n                db = json.load(f)\n            for case in db.get(client_id, []):\n                for error in case.get("errors", []):\n                    if query.lower() in error.get("description", "").lower():\n                        results.append({\n                            "input_data": case.get("input_data", {}),\n                            "output_data": case.get("output_data", {}),\n                            "error": error\n                        })\n        return results\n\n    def improve_input_generation(self, client_id: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        """\n If you keeping encountering errors, unexpected situation, or you are not sure of proceeding, use RoleZero.ask_human to ask for help.\n2. Carefully review your progress at the current task, if your actions so far has not fulfilled the task instruction, you should continue with current task. Otherwise, finish current task by Plan.finish_current_task explicitly.\n3. Each time you finish a task, use RoleZero.reply_to_human to report your progress.\n4. Don\'t forget to append task first when all existing tasks are finished and new tasks are required.\n5. Avoid repeating tasks you have already completed. And end loop when all requirements are met.\nYou are an autonomous programmer\n\nThe special interface consists of a file editor that shows you 100 lines of a file at a time.\n\nYou can use terminal commands (e.g., cat, ls, cd) by calling Terminal.run_command.\n\nYou should carefully observe the behavior and results of the previous action, and avoid triggering repeated errors.\n\nIn addition to the terminal, I also provide additional tools. \n\nIf provided an issue link, you first action must be navigate to the issue page using Browser tool to understand the issue.\n\nYour must check if the repository exists at the current path. If it exists, navigate to the repository path. If the repository doesn\'t exist, please download it and then navigate to it.\nAll subsequent actions must be performed within this repository path. Do not leave this directory to execute any actions at any time.\n\nNote:\n\n1. If you open a file and need to get to an area around a specific line that is not in the first 100 lines, say line 583, don\'t just use the scroll_down command multiple times. Instead, use the Editor.goto_line command. It\'s much quicker. \n2. Always make sure to look at the currently open file and the current working directory (which appears right after the currently open file). The currently open file might be in a different directory than the working directory! Note that some commands, such as \'create\', open files, so they might change the current open file.\n3. When using Editor.edit_file_by_replace, if there is no exact match, take the difference in indentation into consideration.\n4. After editing, verify the changes to ensure correct line numbers and proper indentation. Adhere to PEP8 standards for Python code.\n5. NOTE ABOUT THE EDIT COMMAND: Indentation really matters! When editing a file, make sure to insert appropriate indentation before each line! Ensuring the code adheres to PEP8 standards. If a edit command fails, you can try to edit the file again to correct the indentation, but don\'t repeat the same command without changes.\n6. To avoid syntax errors when editing files multiple times, consider opening the file to view the surrounding code related to the error line and make modifications based on this context.\n7. Ensure to observe the currently open file and the current working directory, which is displayed right after the open file. The open file might be in a different directory than the working directory. Remember, commands like \'create\' open files and might alter the current open file.\n8. Effectively using Use search commands (`search_dir`, `search_file`, `find_file`) and navigation commands (`open_file`, `goto_line`) to locate and modify files efficiently. You may change this if using a different model\n\n    def generate_test_input(self, prompt: str) -> dict:\n        """\n        Sends a prompt to the GPT-4 API and returns the generated test input as a dictionary.\n        The response is expected to be a JSON string representing the test input data.\n        """\n        headers = {\n            "Authorization": f"Bearer {self.api_key}",\n            "Content-Type": "application/json"\n        }\n        payload = {\n            "model": self.model,\n            "messages": [\n                {"role": "system", "content": "You are a helpful assistant that generates test input data for automated test scenarios in JSON format."},\n                {"role": "user", "content": prompt}\n            ],\n            "temperature": 0.2,\n            "max_tokens": 2048\n        }\n\n        response = requests.post(self.api_url, headers=headers, data=json.dumps(payload))\n        if response.status_code != 200:\n            raise Exception(f"GPT-4 API request failed: {response.status_code} {response.text}")\n\n        result = response.json()\n        # Extract the content from the response\n        try:\n            content = result["choices"][0]["message"]["content"]\n            # Try to parse the content as JSON\n            test_input_data = json.loads(content)\n            if not isinstance(test_input_data, dict):\n                raise Exception("GPT-4 response is not a valid JSON object.")\n            return test_input_data\n        except (KeyError, IndexError, json.JSONDecodeError) as e:\n            raise Exception(f"Failed to parse GPT-4 response: {str(e)}")'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of gpt4_api.py has been completed according to the system design, providing the GPT4API class and the generate_test_input method.
